titre: 3 Minute Zilean Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Zillian support? Well, he is an amazing team fighter. He brings tons of lockdown. He is extremely mobile and his ultimate is gamechanging. However, he is weaker in the early game and being locked down does stop him from using his amazing alt. Zillian's passive as he generates experience over time, which he stores up. When he has enough experience that he can level up a teammate, he can then click on them to grant the experience to both them and himself. Zillian's Q throws out a bomb onto the floor, and after 3 seconds, it will explode, dealing damage to nearby enemies. If this is thrown onto any unit, ally or enemy, it sticks to them. Or if they walk through it, they also pick it up. If two bombs are thrown onto the same unit, the first bomb immediately explodes, dealing damage in a circle around it and stunning all nearby enemies. Zillian's W reduces the remaining cooldown of his E and Q by 10 seconds. This is what allows him to throw out two bombs onto the same target. Zillian's E is a point-and-click ability. If it's used on an ally, they're granted a massive amount of movement speed, and if used on an enemy, they're granted a massive slow. Zillian's ultimate is a point-and-click ability which can be used on an ally or himself. If the target is killed within 5 seconds of this being used on them, they're instead resurrected after 3 seconds. For combos, E your opponent into Q, then reset your cool downs with W. Follow up with Q to stun them and then attack away before using E again to slow them. For matchups, Zillian is great into skillshot reliant champions as his high mobility allows him to easily dodge them and he can struggle against champions who can easily engage on him. For runes, take this. Ery makes everything you do better. For build order, start World Atlas and POTS into a battle song and tier 2 boots. After this, an Imperial Mandate and Redemption are great pickups before finishing a build with any of these as needed. For skill order, start Q, then W, then E before maxing Q, then E, then W, taking ult whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and heal. For synergies, Zillian is great with ADCs who lack mobility themselves as he grants them a ton. Starting the game, you do not have the biggest presence in lane yet. Your Q's can be difficult to land, and it's a massive amount of mana to use your Q, WQ stun. If you can play passively until you hit level six, this will skip most of your weaker early game. This is because with Zillian's passive, you and your carry will always hit level six before your opponent. And once you have your ultimate, everything you do becomes incredibly safe. As in a 2v2 fight, your opponents essentially have to kill you guys three times. Other than this, you do want to try and get brush control and still throw QES at your opponent whenever you can. Where Zillian is amazing is in gank setup. Slowing your opponent with E basically guarantees your double Q unless they flash. Between the massive slow, the stun, and another massive slow, your opponents simply can't get away from you. So, as long as you have the advantage, run them down. Entering the mid game, take your tower as soon as possible and rotate mid your carry. Speed yourself up around the map looking for picks and convert into objectives. Entering late game team fights, play the backline support. Your main focus will always be to try and get a good double bomb off to stun as many opponents as possible. Use E on anyone going for your carry or on your carry so they can easily kite. Otherwise, it's all about landing a big revive. So, identify the most important members of your team and focus on reviving them. And that's how to play Zillian Support.

titre: Fix League of Legends stuck on tutorial patching screen
transcription: If your League of Legends is stuck on the tutorial screen here on the patching, then make sure to listen to this tutorial very carefully and I'll guide you on what to do. Now, if your tutorial or patching process looks frozen in League of Legends, don't panic. Open Task Manager to check if it's still downloading. If it is, just let it finish. The user interface or UE is bugged, but the client is actually working in the background. Hopefully, this helps someone save time instead of reinstalling everything. The patch size is around 20 GB. It's not a small update, so it can take some time, especially if you have a slow internet connection. Just wait and ensure your internet connection is working fine. So don't panic if you come here to the screen, it just looks stuck on the tutorial patching. Just you can open here task manager to check if it's downloading. So type task manager on the search bar and then open it. Once you open task manager, you can click here on the network. So click on the network to check if it's downloading. As you can see here, mine is downloading. So, this will depend on your internet connection, but approximately the patch size is around 20 GB. You'll have to wait and the issue will likely resolve by itself. So, don't panic. Hopefully, this helps someone save time instead of reinstalling everything. The patch size is around 20 GB. It's not a small update, so it can take some time, especially if you have a slow internet connection. Just wait and ensure your internet connection is working fine. Thank you so much for watching this video and see you next time.

titre: 3 Minute Anivia Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Anivvia mid? Well, she brings high burst and sustained damage. She brings tons of lockdown. She has amazing zone control, and she has a built-in revive. However, she does lack any mobility, and she is ridiculously mana hungry. Anivvia's passive is when she dies, she becomes an egg. Enemies can attack this, but after 6 seconds, if she has any health left, she is revived. Anivvia's Q has two parts to it. The first is a straight line skill shot which deals damage to enemies hit and slows them. Upon reaching max rank or when recast it explodes in a circle where it deals damage to enemies hit and stuns them. Ania's W summons a wall of ice which knocks units in a direction. This lingers for 5 seconds becoming impossible to rain. And because this can displace enemies, it can count as one proc towards your electrocute. Anivvia's E has a passive where enemies hit by her Q or by a fully stacked ultimate become chilled. The active is a point-and-click ability which deals damage to the target hit and it deals double damage if they were chilled. Anivvia's ultimate places down a circle on the floor which increases in size over 1 and 1/2 seconds. Before fully formed, it deals damage to enemies within and slows them. And when fully formed, it deals triple damage and the slow is much more effective. This ability constantly consumes mana while active. And if recast or if Ania walks too far away or if she's hard CCd, the ability will end. For combos, your go-to is to simply use Q into W and E before attacking. And your all-in combo is use Q into W and alt. Follow up with an attack and E before attacking away. For matchups, Anivvia is great into assassins, easily zoning them off and becoming too hard to get onto and kill. However, she can struggle against enemies who can bully her from outside of her range. For runes, take this. Your combo works very well with electrocute. For build order, start ring and pot and grab an early tier before completing your Rod of Ages and tier 2 boots. From here, complete your archangels and get Leandre's before finishing a build with any of these as needed. For skill order, start Q, then E, then take a second point in E before taking W. From here, max E, then Q, then W, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and teleport. Starting the game, your main goal is to farm hard and trade around your electrocute. You have a very long basic auto attack range, so farming is fairly safe. Otherwise, just look to proc electrocute. From the get- go, an attack into E and attack is a great way to start. If you ever land Q, following up with an attack into E is great. Otherwise, Q into W and E is a great way to chunk your opponent. Otherwise, you're essentially looking to farm hard and get your level six. Getting this prior can be incredibly strong for your team, especially with how quickly you can help take Grubs. Entering the mid game, you can easily hold down a lane, but you do also love team fighting, so either hold mid or push out a side lane and group with your team, getting easy picks with your wards and stuns. Entering late game team fights, play as the backline control mage. In a team fight, your job is to make sure opponents get punished if they ever overextend. titre: Xayah Tips and Tricks That PRO Players Use
transcription: Hey guys, today I'm going to show you the most important Xayah tips and tricks that PRO players use to carry their games. Xayah's Q has more range when you use it on walls. Her 'Q' can deal damage to multiple opponents, but only the first target will take the most damage. So when trading with your opponents, try to hit them directly with 'Q'. You can auto attack and use 'Q' at the same time. This is an effective way to burst an enemy with low health. When you want to combine Flash with 'Q', use 'Q' first and then 'Flash' because the animation is faster. Her 'Q' can be used through walls to deal damage to your opponents. Also, avoid facing walls when using 'Q' on your opponents because the landing position of the feathers is weird. Her 'Q' can be used to check the bushes for enemies. If you hit a hidden enemy there, you will hear a specific sound. When you attack the enemy towers, use Xayah's 'W' because it will increase your attack speed and you will be able to destroy them faster.  When you use 'E' the hitbox of Xayah's feathers is bigger. You can use the AA-Q trick to stun your opponents faster with 'E'. When you want to stun an opponent who is out of range of your auto attacks, use 'Q' on them, then auto attack a minion and then use 'E'. You can use Flash to change the return direction of the feathers so you can stun multiple opponents. When your opponents are under their tower, try to poke them with 'Q' and 'E' because they will be focused to last hit your minions. Once you reach level 3 the basic trading combo is W-AA-Q-AA-E. Xayah's 'R' hitbox is smaller than the spell indicator. You can also use her ultimate through walls to deal damage to your opponents. When you want to combine 'R' with 'E' to stun your opponents, avoid hitting them with the tip of 'R' because sometimes 'E' won't stun the target. When you activate her ultimate, you will only be able to use your summoner spells. When you want to combine Flash with 'R', you can use 'R' out of your opponents' vision and then Flash to surprise them. You can use Xayah's ultimate to dodge enemy abilities. Her ultimate can also be used to check the bushes for enemies. If you hit a hidden enemy, you will see a specific animation. Once you reach level 6 the аll in combo is R-E-W-AA-Q-AA. Also, you can use Galeforce to change the return direction of the feathers so you can stun multiple opponents. When you use Stopwatch, you will not be able to recast 'E' until the Stopwatch effect ends. In team fights stay in the back line of your team and try to deal as much damage as possible to the closest enemies. Also, try to hit as many enemies as possible with your ultimate. If this video was useful to you, don't forget to like it.

titre: 5 WAYS TO IMPROVE YOUR SONA GAMEPLAY! | League of Legends
transcription: Here are five helpful tips that you might not know about Sona. Number one, auto attack reset. You can get a faster auto attack off if you have your power cord. You can auto attack, press Q, and then auto attack again instantly. This is insanely helpful in small fights and when you want to destroy a ward quicker. Number two, stacking her passive. Did you know you can actually get two stacks when you cast a W? The heal is one stack and the second stack is from the shield. Your ally has to take damage to the shields you provide them. And it has to be meaningful damage. It can't just be one or two damage. Which leads us into number three, insane ultimate cooldown. When your passive is fully stacked, every stack you obtain that would count towards your passive now reduces your ultimate cool down by 1.5 seconds. You can maximize this effects by shielding multiple of your allies on an objective like Baron Nasher. You're likely to get five or six stacks instantly. As Baron does enough damage to the shields to count as a stack, so your ultimate is instantly reduced by 10 or 12 seconds. Every cast of your Q can also gain two stacks if it hits two enemies, reducing your cool down by a further 3 seconds. Number four, Q aura. Aside from sending out her damaging bolts to enemies, Sona also makes her allies deal bonus magic damage on their next basic attack within the next 5 seconds. A lot of players don't know this. And number five, aura effects. If you have your summon area available, it will actually attach to your ally when they are affected by your Q or E aura. Aries shields your ally which will also proc your echo of helilia, dream maker, counterwards, dynams effects, and also spreads auras like ardent sensor and staff of flowing water. Thanks so much for watching and also check out my other Sona videos on my channel. See you soon. Bye.

If both of these instances of damage hit an enemy, that's an eight-second mantra cooldown reduction. titre: 3 Minute Senna Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Senna bot? Well, she is an infinitely scaling carry. She brings great sustain, her range is ridiculously long, and her global ultimate is game-changing. However, she does lack mobility before her items, and she is skillshot reliant for a lot of her kit. Senna's first passive is all of her basic attacks are not projectiles. However, when she buys attack speed, she gains reduced effects. Her second passive are her infinitely scaling souls, which you can get in multiple ways. Attacking or landing an ability on an enemy champion will apply a stack. If she does this again within 4 seconds, she does bonus damage and gains a permanent soul. Also, enemies that die nearby have a chance to drop a soul, and she can attack them or hit them with Q or alt to collect them. For each soul, she gains permanent attack damage, and every 20 souls, she gains bonus attack, range, and crit strike chance. And her third passive is all of her basic attacks just simply deal bonus damage and grant her bonus move speed. Senna's Q is a point-and-click ability which can be used on an enemy, ally, or anything else. And then when used, it shoots out a long-range rectangular skill shot which deals damage to enemies hit and slows them while also healing herself and all ally champions. The cooldown of this ability is reduced every time she attacks. Senna's W is a straight line skill shot which deals damage to the first enemy hit and attaches to them. After a second, or if the target dies, then them and all enemies around them get rooted. Senna's E creates a circle around her where she camouflages herself and all ally champions within. All allies within become camouflaged and gain bonus move speed. Senna's ultimate is a massive global straight line skill shot. It deals damage to all enemies hit by the center and in a much larger area grants a shield to Senna and all her allies. For combos, start with E if needed into an attack, Q, W, and ult before attacking away more. And if you start with W, attack into Q and ult before attacking away. For matchups, Senna is great into less aggressive laners where she can simply farm her passive. And she can struggle into harder lane bullies who won't let her scale. For runes, take this. fleet for movement speed, damage, and healing is just amazing synergy. For build order, start blade and pot into a ghost blade and tier 2 boots. From here, grab an RFC and an infge before finishing a build with any of these. For skill order, start Q, then W, then E before maxing Q, then W, then E, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and barrier. For synergy, she works well with champions who bring her even more peel. Starting the game, you're looking to scale into a beast of a hyper carry, so focus on farming while poking and harassing your opponent. Your attack range being 600 is great as it means you just simply outrange almost everyone. So if you can ever attack the same target twice or simply attack and queue them, not only is it great damage, but you've also got a passive stack. Otherwise, landing a W is a great way to start a trade as you can simply just shoot them from out of their range while they have to stand still. And of course, once you get ultimate, look to influence fights around the map, turning the fights for your team. Entering the mid game, take your tower as soon as possible and rotate mid. This puts two strong champions in the center of the map to quickly roam to fights and objectives while sieging the most important mid outer tower. Entering late game team fights, play as the backline hyper carry. Use your ultimate early in the fight over as many allies and enemies as possible for maximum value. From here, rely on we E and your long attack range to keep yourself safe while spamming out QES on as many allies and enemies as possible.

titre: 3 Minute Renekton Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Renekton top? Well, he has very high base damage. He has great built-in sustain. He is a massive lane bully and he's very mobile for a bruiser. However, he does have high cool downs in the early game and he can get kited if he can't land his first E. Renekton's passive is instead of having mana, he has fury. He gains fury when he attacks and starts losing it when he hasn't generated any for 12 seconds. While he has at least 50 fury, his next basic attack is empowered and he generates bonus fury when below half health. Renekton's Q deals damage in a circle around him and heals himself for each enemy hit. And for each enemy champion hit, the healing is increased. In addition, he gains a small amount of fury for each non-chion hit and a large amount for each champion hit. And when this ability is empowered, it deals bonus damage and triples the healing. Renekton's W is an auto attack reset, which makes his next attack strike twice and deal modified damage and stun the target. And when this ability is empowered, he strikes three times instead, destroys shields, and stuns for even longer. Renekton's E causes him to dash a fixed distance, dealing damage to enemies he passes through. If this hits any enemy, he can then recast it in the next 4 seconds. And when this ability is empowered, the second part of it deals bonus damage, and reduces the target's armor. Renekton's ultimate is a massive steroid which lasts for 15 seconds. It grants him bonus health, increased size, bonus attack range, and 20 fury. During this time, he deals damage in a large circle around him, and generates fury over time. For combos, your go-to is to E into an attack and W. Attack into Q before attacking into your second E. Your all-in combo is to attack into W before attacking into E. Once more, attack into Q before attacking into your second E. And this combo also works well if you alt after using W. For matchups, Renekton works well into most other mely top laners just having better stats than them. However, he can struggle against champions that just sit outside of his E range. For runes, take this. Conqueror has amazing synergy. For build order, start blade and pot into an eclipse and tier 2 boots. From here, a black lever into Sterax are great pickups before finishing a build with any of these. For skill order, start Q, then E, then W before maxing Q, then E, then W, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and teleport. Although, ignite is a good go-to if you want to kill your opponent. From the start of the game, Renekton is a very strong lane bully in the top lane. So, you want to use your great stats, great sustain, and the fact you have no mana to your advantage. Spend the first couple of levels stacking fury, and then once you have all three abilities, you can go in. If you can walk up to your opponent, simply attack into your stun. This saves both your E to keep chasing them down. Otherwise, use the first E to go in and stun them and then use the second one to either chase them down or just simply back out. And you want to do this as often as possible around your cooldowns. And once you hit level six, this becomes even scarier. Entering the mid game, you want to take your tower as soon as possible and keep it pushed out. This will give you plenty of time to look for skirmishes, which you love to fight. Entering late game team fights, play as your front line, diving their backline. Renekton's kit is fully set up to be able to get onto the enemy backline. Use your first E to dive through their front line to either get to their back line or have your second E to dash afterwards. From here, you ultimate, stun them, and just start smashing damage into them while your team very easily follows up. And that's how you play the crocodile top lane. powerful concept related to the first Scuttle spawn that you must know as a jungler here I'm in a Smurf commentary specifically teaching how to play the first Scuttle spawns so when I take this Scuttle I'm already pushing it bside so I can get to the next one as fast as possible then I have no interest in fighting Mal fight because I know that getting both scuttles has way more value notice how I get to Mid before Mal fight into then getting botside before Mal fight when you double Scuttle the enemy jungler you effectively get ahead two camps on them while also being able to gank two lanes before them now obviously in that game there was no ganks available but if you go back to the original clip you can see he had no gang bot either his bot Lane was pushed up this is what's so important to understand it's not just about your ganks it's that you get to block the enemy's ganks as well if you went for the double Scuttle then carus not only doesn't get the Scuttle Bots side but it also prevents him from ganking his bot Lane and that's not the only benefit remember what you learned earlier if you double Scuttle you can Spike six off one void grub after your second rotation okay so why is understanding the value of the double Scuttle so important well let me show you here he's finishing his full clear gets the topside Scuttle has the ability to double Scuttle the enemy but looks to gank to instead gets countered by the enemies in and goes from having a huge advantage to being behind next game finishes his full clear gets the Scuttle looks to gank top instead of double scuttling and yeah once again playing from behind instead of ahead different game full clears gets the top Scuttle but now it's a tower dive unnecessary risk when he has the guaranteed double Scuttle this one is especially devastating makes the correct call to peel off his camps early to counter top gets a kill into nearly a double kill now has a great transition to double Scuttle the enemy jungler but goes for the invade instead and yeah once again we're playing from behind so now you see just how important it is to know the value of taking two scuttles and I have some good news you can actually learn everything you need to know about how to play around these early scuttles in like 2 minutes we have a perfect guide on it in Chapter 3 of our jungle course check it out all right now let's get into how to deal with those first Scuttle spawns this can be a source of a lot of frust ation for players often going for a Scuttle only for none of your teammates to help while the enemy jungler's teammates collapse on you so let's fix that there's only two scenarios you need to know of first if both junglers path away from each other not much has to be said both scuttles are free for both junglers at the end of their routes second the real problem when both junglers are pathing in the same direction you only have three options so it's easy to memorize option one you check your nearby lanes and they have Lane priority so for example let's say I'm finishing my route and I see both my top and mid have Lane priority great always go for that Scuttle of course if you have a strong 1V one matchup that you feel you easily win you can fight regardless if your laners are reacting otherwise here's the trick don't just immediately fight the enemy jungler expecting laners to collapse ping early on and don't commit to any fight if the enemy jungler is contesting you over the Scuttle you want to wait for your laners to react first and then start fighting now you're probably thinking yeah my laners never react even when they have Lane priority well if this happens and you feel that you lose the 1 V one against the enemy jungler all you have to do is cross through mid and take the off opposite Scuttle this prevents you from being double scuttled by the enemy jungler as they'll be busy taking the Scuttle on the other side of the map so option one you have Lane priority in both nearby Lanes you always go for it and if you have a weak match up play it slow waiting for them if they still don't move head to the other Scuttle now option two you have Lane priority in one nearby Lane but lack it in the other nearby Lane like this example here I have Lane priority top but lack Lane priority in mid what do you do then well the trick is to spam ping early on and watch both lanes to see who reacts first while you're doing your last cam if your top doesn't move while the enemy mid disappears or starts moving then you give up the Scuttle however if like in this case you see your teammate is the one reacting first while the enemy isn't moving well you go for the Scuttle as you can see it makes for easy kills on the enemy jungler now option three what if the enemy was the one to react first or you simply lack Lane priority in both lanes well you want to recall spend your gold to get an item advantage and then head to the bside Scuttle here's a great example I'm finishing up my clear and I'm pathing to the same side as the enemy jungler despite having Lane priority top I'm lacking it in mid my top hasn't warded and it isn't moving and Corky appears from mid all signs for me to give up the Scuttle so I instantly Recall now while the enemy jungler is busy tops side I'm the one with the item advantage since I recalled this guarantees me the botside Scuttle and prevents me from being double scuttled you may be wondering why recall though can't we just run straight to the bot Scuttle you can but it's often better to recall for two reasons first it lets you rotate from the Scuttle to the first Camp that's respawning in your second rotation and go straight into your next clear if you don't recall the rotation feels horrible since you haven't spent your gold yet and so you'll be doing your second rotation sitting on a ton of gold a really bad mistake to avoid second it sets a trap on the enemy jungler for example here you can see I'm finishing up my clear I lack Lane priority top but the enemy is so low it's basically a free kill the problem is I have to help my top clear this wave if I leave him Zach's just going to kill him because I did all this Zach is going to have enough time to get to the Scuttle before me and smite it so I then recall giving up the top Scuttle here's where the Trap comes in again the enemy jugler hasn't recalled so we have an item Advantage so be on the lookout if they try to gank mid the counter gank from you hits incredibly hard with the early item Advantage basically guaranteeing it will go well and there you go you now have everything you need to form a game plan around the first Scuttle spawns great so now you know the game plan he was taught as well let's see how it worked for him in his games after he learned this here he's full clearing he notices he lacks Lane priority top and it even looks like the enemy Riven was starting to move into the river so he makes the right call and instantly recalls now he gets the skuttle botside preventing himself from getting double scuttled and heads into his next clear with two amp TOS then at the end of a second clear he spots Teemo ganking mid there's two great things at work here first he gets to countergank this Teemo in his dead time which is super efficient secondly he spent his gold on that initial recall whereas teimo hasn't recalled yet so he's up two ampts right now and so that's a pretty easy win when you're up 40 ability power in stats now he's taking void grubs in his Deadtime and since we made sure to secure at least one Scuttle combined with that XP we got from killing Teemo that means we hit level six off two void grubs this completely catches Teemo off guard and yeah that's another free kill still there was one big final problem that needed to be solved in this game he gets a leash and full clears towards top side now I want you to take a look at when he finishes his clear 3 minutes and 23 seconds here's the Lilia clear we teach on our website notice how in this one we don't even get a leash yet we're able to finish our clear by 3 minutes and 11 seconds that's 12 seconds faster the reason I bring this up is that he makes the right call going for the Scuttle he has Lane priority in mid and top but his clear speed is just a little bit too slow where the enemy gets to the Scuttle first and he has no time to wait for his Lane priority to kick in if his clear was just a bit faster he would have been able to double Scuttle the nocturn giving him a huge lead similar issue here where he full clears but then doesn't instantly recall this combined with a slower clear time means despite making the right choice in this game giving up the Scuttle recalling and heading to the bot Scuttle he still gets double scuttled despite having Lane priority bot and being able to easily win that fight if he got there earlier that's why we created an entire course on mastering your specific chance Champions jungle clears since if your clear is too slow you're not able to generate leads even when you make the right choice so obviously I linked him the Lilia clear from the course and that was no longer an issue moving forward now it's very important for you to understand this next lesson so here I'm on a platinum account so I can teach the concepts you just learned I'm against a Gres jungle take and ignite I full clear and as I get top I have the unfortunate matchup of smolder vers Pantheon yeah there's no way I'm going for that Scuttle so I recall just like I taught you back pinging my teammates while I do I head head to the botside Scuttle and look to hit a really efficient gank botside before I enter into my next clear we end up getting Ash's flash sweet back into my next clear I go and what do you know by the end of it my smolder is about to die my mid laner has no Lane priority so yeah once again I have to recall this time giving up the void grubs naturally smolder dies because it's smolder top against Pantheon so now we're botside this lets me block a gank on my mid laner from the enemy Pantheon we then get the Scuttle there and remember that Ash whose flash we got earlier well now we cash in on that ganking her setting up to kills and since I have the number Advantage from my bot Lane I start ding Graves mistakenly contest but I have a number Advantage along with being level six from farming more efficiently and yeah suddenly this game isn't looking so bad so there's two reasons I showed you this first is that it does not matter how good you are you can't force a winning early game as a jungler you have to adapt to what's given to you second is that when you do adapt that often means giving things up in the short term I had to give up the top Scuttle after my first clear and I gave up void grubs after my second clear it wasn't until well past 7 Minutes in the game before I finally got an opportunity to actually start snowballing and this is me the guy in the YouTube video teaching you right now let alone what it will be like in your own games when you try to do this what I'm trying to say is a good amount of games you play as a jungler you're going to have to be really patient and focus on minimizing losses too often I see junglers I coach Force things early on feeling like if they don't the game is going to snowball out of control but they don't realize the reason the games they're playing are snowballing out of control is because they're forcing things sometimes highle jungling is literally literally full clearing doing a measly little gank on bot into a second full clear into then finally getting something of value and believe it or not I actually won that game despite my Top Lane smolder literally intentionally feeding going to an 18 if you want to see how I did it the full game with commentary is live right now in our companion course for this guide where we also have the next six episodes already live for you to watch I go over how to play around the early objectives like dragons Rift tailed and void grubs the specific techniques to use that makes using your camera to check Lanes way titre: 3 Minute Mordekaiser Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Mordekaiser's a top? Well, his ultimate basically guarantees kills. He shreds through both tanks and squishies. He's one of the best one v two champions in the game and is also a manaless champion. However, he does lack any mobility at all and he is very skill shot reliant on his combo. Mord's first passive are his basic attacks empowered to deal bonus damage on hit. His second passive is every time he damages an enemy champion or large monster with an attack or ability, he gains a stack. At three stacks, he gains a small amount of bonus movement speed and creates a circle around him which constantly deals damage to enemies within. Mordekaiser's Q is a cone skill shot where after a short delay, he slams his mace on the ground dealing damage to enemies within. And if it hits only one enemy, the damage is massively increased. Mordekaiser's W has a passive where he stores a large amount of the damage he deals and a small amount of the damage he takes as a white bar underneath his health. The active converts the white bar into a shield for himself scaling with how large the white bar was. This ability can also be recast to convert a portion of the remaining shield into healing. Mordekaiser's E has a passive where he just passively gains magic pen. The active is a large rectangular skill shot which then after a short delay summons a claw within it to pull enemies in that direction dealing damage to them. Mordekaiser's ultimate is a point and click ability which briefly slows the enemy before banishing them and him to the death realm for seven seconds. While in the death realm, Mordekaiser heals himself for 10% of the max health and reduces their ability power, attack speed, health, armor, magic resist, and size and attack damage. All of these are reduced by 10% and Mordekaiser instead gains all these stats. Then if he kills them while in the death realm, he keeps these stats until they respawn. For combos, your go to is to attack into Q before attacking into E and attacking away. When engaging, simply land E into your Q and attack away. And your all in is to E into your ultimate and a Q before attacking away. And in all these combos, use your W and fully stacked for the massive shield. For match ups, Mord's great into less mobile tanky top laners easily landing his abilities. And he can struggle against high mobile champions who dodge them. For runes, take this. Conqueror gives him everything he wants. For build order, start with ring and pots into crystal scepter and tier two boots. After this, I riftmaker and liandry's and grievous pickups before finishing a build with any of these. For skill order, start Q then E then W before maxing Q then E then W taking ult whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and ignite. Starting the game as the top laner, your main focus is going to be on farming hard, completing your quest as soon as possible, and taking good trades. At levels one and two, you're incredibly strong as your passive is very strong if you can just hit them three times. Otherwise, you're very happy to poke with Q where available or just sit back and farm. When you hit level six and have access to your ultimate, now's when you want to fight. Then if there's ever a window for a good fight, you can just force your opponent into it. Entering the mid game, you're very strong when you've got your first couple of items. Take your tower as soon as possible. Then you can either split push as you are a very strong duelist. And if they send multiple people to stop you, you force them into one v ones with your ult. Or you can simply push out and group back with your team. Entering late game team fights, play the front to back play style. This is essentially where you attack the enemy front line until they're gone, then you can go for the back line. However, Mord's main skill expression is in his ultimate. If they've got a fed hyper carry or assassin, take them out the fight. Or if they've got a big engage champion going in, just simply ult them.

titre: 3 Minute Yasuo Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Yasuo top? Well, he brings amazing constant damage. He is very mobile. His kit counters projectile champions and he has massive outplay potential. However, he does take some time to master and he is damage reliant so can struggle from behind. Yaso's first passive is whenever he buys crit strike chance, he gains double the amount, and anything over 100% grants him attack damage. His second passive is when he moves, he generates his bar. When the bar is full, the next time he takes damage from a champion or monster, he gains a shield. Yasawo's Q is a short-range straightline skill shot, which deals damage to all targets hit and grants him a stack. Once he has two stacks, his third Q becomes a longer range straightline skill shot, which also knocks up enemies. Yaso's W throws out a wall, which blocks and deletes all enemy projectiles. Yaso's E is a point-and-click ability, which dashes him a set distance to that enemy, dealing damage to them. This ability has a very short cool down, and when used, it empowers the next one to deal 25% more damage, which can stack up to four times. In addition, if Yaso's Q is used mid dash, it instead deals a circle of damage around him. And if this is the third Q, it still knocks up. Yaso's ultimate can only be used if a nearby enemy champion is airborne. Upon use, his secondary bar is instantly filled, and he knocks up all enemy champions, dealing damage to them. Then, for the next 15 seconds, damage dealt by Yaso's crits ignore a massive amount of the targets bonus armor. For combos, start with your third Q already charged. Then, use E and Q. Make sure to attack before using alt and then attack again into Q and more attacks. And if you land your third Q from range, ult into an attack and Q. Attack into E before attacking and queuing away. For matchups, Yaso is great into range top laners, blocking key abilities and easily getting onto them, and he can struggle against some of the harder lane bullies that you'll find in top. For runes, take this. Lethal tempo along with green tree makes you an amazing jewelist. For build order, start blade and pot into tier 2 boots and a bork. From here, a shield bow and i.e. They're great pickups before finishing your build with any of these as needed. For skill order, start Q then E then W before maxing Q then E then W, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and teleport. While a lot of Yasuos will be tempted by ignite over teleport, the top lane Yaso does tend to be more of a split pusher, so teleport is important. Starting the game, as always, you're looking to farm hard and take good trades. From the first couple of levels, Yasuo can play aggressive. Having access to multiple short cooldown Q's as well as a resetting E is just great aggression. However, Yasuo's Q cooldown is reliant on his attack speed. So, if you can get your Berserker's grease on your first back, you're in a ridiculously strong position. Otherwise, just keep stacking your Q and then looking to play aggressive when it's stacked. Enter in the mid game. You want to take your tower as soon as possible. From here, Yasuo is a strong split pusher. So, push the wave out as far as you can safely and then either look for towers and fights or group up with your team. Entering late game team fights, you are the hyper carry, but how you play depends on your team. If your team has easy access to knockups, wait for them to engage and then follow up. Otherwise, play a frontto-back play style where you just attack anything in front of you, then wait your chance to get on their backline before diving

titre: League of Legends Stuck on Tutorial Patching Screen
transcription: When you install League of Legends, sometimes you might get stuck on the tutorial patching screen. The patching button might appear grayed out and it looks like nothing is happening. This isn't actually an error. The game is still downloading in the background. You can check this using task manager. Click on the network column header to sort the list by the highest usage. The process showing active network usage is the real patch. The patch size is about 20 GB. This is a large update, so it can take a while, especially if you have a slow internet connection. You'll need to wait for it to finish. There's no way to speed up the download process itself because it depends on your internet speed. However, you can try resetting your internet modem. This can refresh your internet connection. If you're lucky, you might see that your internet speed is much faster now. As you can see on this computer, the internet speed is more than 50 times faster. This means the patching will finish much more quickly. The patching button has now disappeared and we can start the game as usual. That's it. Thanks for watching. Don't forget to like and subscribe and see you in the next

titre: Viego Tips and Tricks That PRO Players Use
transcription: In today's video, I will show you the most important Viego tips and tricks that pro players use to carry their games. The range of Viego's auto attacks is greater than most melee champions in the game. You can even attack your opponents through thin walls. Viego's 'Q' hit box is bigger than the spell indicator. When farming in the jungle, try to pull the monsters and line them up in a straight line so you can hit more at once with 'Q'. You can use Viego's 'Q' through walls to deal damage to your opponents. His 'Q' can be combined with 'Flash', but first use 'Q', then 'Flash' because the animation is faster. You can use 'Q' and 'W' at the same time. This is an effective way to burst an enemy with low hp. The longer you charge Viego's 'W', the greater the range of the stun becomes. Also the duration of the stun increases. But the range of the dash does not increase. Unfortunately, you can't go through walls with Viego's 'W'. But you can still deal damage to your opponents. With Viego's 'W' you can reset your auto attacks to attack your opponents faster. The easiest way to hit your 'W' is when your enemy is near a wall, because he will have less space to move. You can also use his 'W' to dodge enemy abilities or to escape from your opponents. You can combine Flash with Viego's 'W' to close the gap. If you want to kill a low hp opponent who is far away from you, you can use the combo W-Q-Flash. Viego's 'W' range is exactly the same as the 'E' range in which opponents can see you. Use this indicator to hit your 'W' every time, when you engage on your opponents. You can use 'W' and 'E' at the same time to hide the 'W' animation. This is an effective way to stun and surprise your opponent. I you use 'E' on the minions, you will be able to go through them for a short period of time. When you attack the enemy towers, use Viego's 'E' because it will increase your attack speed and you will be able to destroy them faster. Before you start recaling, use Viego's 'E'. The duration of 'E' is exactly the same as the recal duration. And when you're at the base, you can use 'W' and 'E' to go faster to the other lanes. If you use Viego's 'E' near a bush and if there is a hidden enemy there, you will get a sign. Once you reach level 3 the basic trading combo is E-W-AA-Q-AA. The hitbox of Viego's ultimate is smaller than the spell's indicator. When you use your ultimate on multiple opponents, it will only deal the most damage to the closest opponent you used your ultimate on. But if there is an opponent with low health, your ultimate will focus on dealing the most damage to him. Viego's Ultimate has a big range, you can use it to go through some big walls. You can also combine Flash with his Ultimate to close the gap. Or you can use Flash-W-R if your opponent is further away from you. You can also use his ultimate to try and dodge enemy abilities. When you possess an enemy body, the cooldown of Viego's ultimate resets. But be careful when possessing enemy bodies under their tower, because the aggro from the tower won't reset. Once you reach level 6 the аll in combo is E-W-AA-Q-AA-R-AA When playing with conqueror and you have 12 stacks, the more opponents you hit with 'Q' and 'R', the more you will heal. In team fights, if the enemy team has a lot of CC, wait for your team to engage. Also look for opportunities to flank the enemy team and find the squishy enemy champions. Try to prioritize possessing the tanky champions, because if you possess the squishy champions, you'll die faster. If this video was useful to you, don't forget to like it.

titre: TEMPO GUIDE - HOW TO GET CHALLENGER IN LEAGUE OF LEGENDS
transcription:
Aujourd'hui est le jour
où vous allez passer un cap sur League of Legends, parce qu'on va aborder
le concept le plus important du plus haut niveau, mais aussi le plus compliqué du jeu, la tempo. Oui, oui, ce mot magique parfait pour paraître intelligent
en parlant de League of Legends, que vous avez sûrement
entendu plein de fois... *Tem-po* Eh bien, je vais vous
l'expliquer et vous le vulgariser. Mais attention, ça ne sera pas simple. Et plus la vidéo avance,
plus elle sera intense. Vous l'aurez compris. Bienvenue dans le Guide Tempo. Pourquoi un Guide Tempo ? La plupart des joueurs pensent que perdre une game
est due à une faute mécanique, alors que dans beaucoup de cas, c'est parce que tu n'es pas
au bon endroit, au bon moment. Et c'est ça, la tempo. Savoir quoi faire, c'est bien, mais savoir quand et où agir,
c'est encore mieux. Et la tempo, ça existe et concerne
toutes les games de tous les élos, sans que vous le sachiez vraiment. Mais alors, il va falloir vite expliquer :
"C'est quoi la tempo ?" Explique ! Les gars, c'est un peu chiant, mais en gros, la tempo,
c'est un concept qui... Oh, ça va, on rigole. Allez, la tempo, c'est un avantage de timing. En gros, c'est être à un endroit plus tôt ou faire une action
plus vite que ton adversaire. Et en ce faisant,
cela t'offre plus d'options sur la carte et un meilleur contrôle
pendant un moment défini. C'était long, c'était compliqué... C'est normal, écoute les exemples. Si tu back avant ton adversaire, cela te permet de revenir
sur ta lane avant lui, push puis faire une rotation, par exemple. Autre exemple, si tu reset
en même temps que ton jungler  en tant que support, tu peux impacter la carte à deux
avec une supériorité numérique. Et dernier exemple,
si tu arrives en premier sur une position, tu peux ward et clear la vision ennemie. Voilà trois exemples simples qui te montrent des résultats concrets que tu peux tirer en ayant une bonne tempo et qui arrivent toutes tes games sans que tu ne saches le reproduire. En résumé, la tempo, c'est un avantage de timing sur tes adversaires. Et avoir la tempo, c'est être en avance sur le rythme de la partie. C'est donc être celui qui contrôle le jeu. Et ne pas avoir la tempo, c'est être en retard sur les actions adverses, ce qui amène souvent
à être celui qui subit la game. Mais avant d'expliquer
pourquoi la tempo c'est si broken, parlons des différents types de tempo. Parce que pour mieux
comprendre le principe de tempo, on peut et on va la diviser
en deux grandes parties, puis trois sous catégories. La tempo early game et la tempo mid game puis la tempo de lane,
la tempo de la jungle et la tempo d'équipe. Par exemple, push,
reset et revenir en premier, c'est de la tempo de lane d'early game. C'est une action individuelle de début de partie qui te donne de la tempo
sans dépendre de qui que ce soit. Push une wave canon en side à 20min de jeu et chercher un flank mid, c'est de la tempo indiv, mid game ou plus. Quand le jungler adverse fait
un gank après son premier clear, ça te donne la tempo pour invade
son respawn de deuxième clear. C'est donc early game
et individuel en jungle. Skip un cadran
pour invade et jouer des camps, ou jouer la T2 top ou bot, c'est mid game +
et c'est une tempo de jungle. Reset jungle/supp
pour jouer les larves au spawn, c'est une tempo d'équipe d'early game et arriver à 4 au drake 30 secondes avant son spawn pour prendre la vision, poke et catch, c'est une tempo d'équipe mid game ou plus. Le but de ce tableau, c'était de comprendre que
chaque action individuelle ou en équipe peut être classée
et à son impact et ses conséquences sur le rythme de la partie. Donc une action peut te donner de la tempo, t'en faire perdre ou l'utiliser. Tu vas comprendre. Il faut voir la tempo un peu comme une pièce de monnaie
à une salle d'arcade. Reprenons l'exemple du jungler adverse qui fait un gank après son premier clear. En ce faisant, il retarde sa prochaine action sur la carte et son deuxième clear, ce qui te donne en tant
que jungler un token de tempo que tu peux utiliser
dans plusieurs bornes d'arcade différentes. La première, la borne d'arcade invade, voler le deuxième scuttle
ou voler un camp adverse. La deuxième, la borne d'arcade dive et d'aller gank et dive un laner à l'opposé du jungler adverse tranquillement vu qu'il s'est show bot. Mais si tu utilises ta pièce dans une borne, tu ne pourras plus l'utiliser dans l'autre. En effet, si tu dive, il aura eu le temps de faire le scuttle ou de venir défendre ses camps. Et si tu prends le deuxième scuttle
ou son respawn de camp, il aura le temps de défendre le dive. Et tu peux aussi décider
de ne pas utiliser ton token, le conserver, faire ton deuxième full clear et l'utiliser plus tard
pour faire une autre action, comme par exemple un dragon pendant que le jungler adverse
finira son deuxième clear. Mais attention,
c'est difficile de conserver la tempo donc vaut mieux l'utiliser
correctement tant que tu l'as, sinon tu risques de perdre ta pièce ou de te la faire voler
si d'autres actions ont lieu qui changent le cours de la game,
comme par exemple un solo kill. C'est comme ça qu'on peut vulgariser et mieux comprendre la tempo. Des actions de ta part ou de tes adversaires te donneront ou t'enlèveront des tokens de tempo, et toi, tu pourras les utiliser dans diverses actions tout au cours de la partie. Mais les tokens périment vite
et sont à utilisation unique. T'as compris ? Parfait parce que maintenant,
on va pouvoir voir pourquoi la tempo est-elle si importante. Résumons rapidement. Avoir la tempo,
c'est donc un avantage de timing sur la carte et c'est pouvoir contrôler
le jeu via une action unique, individuelle ou collective. Dans le déroulement d'une game,
il y a deux styles d'objectifs. Ceux qui ont un timer d'apparition précis, comme le dragon, les larves,
l'Herald ou le Nashor, et ceux qui n'en ont pas
comme jouer la T2 top, investir la vision botside,
invade un cadran adverse. À partir du mid game, on veut avoir la tempo
pour jouer ces objectifs. Et si on prend l'exemple d'un dragon à jouer, l'équipe qui détient la tempo arrivera en avance, posera de la vision pour avoir des rotations adverses, trap ou catch. Bref, le jeu est plus simple quand tu as la tempo. Et si t'as pas la tempo, tu vas sortir de base à 5,
avec 10sec avant le spawn de la Soul. Et c'est toujours là que ton top va se dire que c'est le bon moment
de push une wave inutile à l'opposé parce qu'il a sa TP alors qu'on avait besoin de lui pour facecheck mais t'inquiète pas, ton ADC avait déjà décidé
de facecheck le jungle par lui-même TOUT SEUL et de se faire exterminer plutôt que rester à 5 et passer par le même endroit
pour contester l'objectif via la mid lane, par exemple. Ouais... Si vous n'avez pas la tempo ni la vision pour contester un objectif très important, allez-y à 5 ensemble par une lane. Cependant, avoir la tempo ne te garantit pas
de gagner un fight, loin de là. Si l'équipe en face de toi
a 10 000 golds d'avance, ils vont forcer l'entrée à 5
et vous massacrer sans considération. Par contre, cela t'offre
des courts timings de jeu pour chunk, surprendre ou catch tes adversaires. De plus, certaines compositions ne dépendent pas d'avoir la tempo pour fonctionner, comme par exemple
une composition teamfight groupée hard engage. Par contre,
une compo poke va toujours vouloir être nanopixel sur la tempo pour avoir le temps de développer son jeu de poke et de ward pour éviter les flancs. Et petit tips, il ne faut pas arriver quand le drake
ou le Nashor spawn, non non. Il faut arriver 45secs
environ avant son spawn pour commencer la guerre
de vision, de position, de poke et de catch autour de l'objectif. Mais une issue de secours existe
quand une équipe n'a pas du tout la tempo. Si ce n'est pas un objectif obligatoire comme le dragon ancestral ou le Nashor, les bons joueurs vont essayer de crossmap et ne vont donc pas contester
un objectif trop bien setup par les adversaires lorsqu'ils sont
en retard de tempo, aka hors tempo. Et c'est comme ça
que vous pouvez récupérer la tempo quand vous ne l'avez pas, avec du crossmap. Sachez aussi que
la tempo est définie par les waves, donc avoir le push sur une lane signifie généralement avoir la tempo parce que ton adversaire devra
attendre sous tour pour clear ou sacrifier sa wave pour te suivre. Et cette notion est essentielle
pour bien maîtriser la tempo, surtout à partir du mid game. Ton jungler va invade,
il faut avoir des prio. Avoir des prio, c'est avoir la tempo. Avoir la tempo, c'est créer du jeu. Et c'est pour ça
que les assassins adorent skip des waves pour avoir la tempo, pour créer du jeu. Et ça, c'est une stratégie qu'on verra après. De plus, la tempo change de manière drastique deux fois dans la game : à 15 minutes puis à 25 minutes. Pourquoi, vous allez me dire ? Eh bien, parce qu'à ces deux timings, c'est lorsqu'on passe d'une wave canon
toutes les trois waves, à une wave canon toutes les deux waves, puis à des waves canons en continu. Et un canon peut tank
7 coups de tour avant de mourir, ce qui signifie beaucoup
plus de temps pour jouer sur la carte pendant que ton adversaire clear la wave. Finalement, on peut s'imaginer ça comme une musique. Au début, on chill,
pas beaucoup d'occasion de jeu, puis le rythme augmente. Possibilité de faire une action sur la carte toutes les deux waves de sbires. Et enfin, c'est le cauchemar. La musique est à son pic. On peut dive, invade, flank,
bouger à chaque wave. Et en plus de tout cela,
depuis la saison 16, les waves spawnent
toutes les 25sec au lieu de 30 à partir de 14 minutes et toutes les 20sec à partir de 30 minutes, ce qui te laisse encore moins de temps
pour utiliser cette tempo entre chaque wave. Vous l'aurez compris, League of Legends, c'est un jeu
où chaque partie se compte en minutes et où chaque seconde fait la différence. Maintenant vous savez reconnaître
quand vous avez la tempo, quoi faire quand vous ne l'avez pas et quand est-ce que c'est essentiel ou non de l'avoir. Donc il ne manque qu'une seule chose. Voyons ensemble
quelles actions permettent justement de voler de la tempo,
d'en gagner et d'en perdre. Allez, c'est parti,
on va enchaîner ensemble des exemples
de solutions connues et moins connues pour manipuler la tempo d'une game. Mais attends un peu, il est temps d'accélérer le rythme de cette vidéo. On arrive dans le haut niveau, c'est à vous de me suivre,
pas à moi de ralentir. Gain de tempo : Choisir de recall au bon moment, par exemple après un kill
ou après une rotation ennemie plutôt que rester push une wave de trop, ça te permet de revenir avant ou en même temps que ton adversaire et redévelopper ton jeu sans que lui ait l'occasion
de roam ou de faire quoi que ce soit. Tuer ton adversaire ou le forcer à reset, c'est un énorme timing de jeu gratuit. Tu peux reset et économiser ta tempo comme on vient de le voir, ou alors tu peux invade,
roam ou juste faire un dragon comme le fait ici Hans Sama. Mais il est obligé de retourner bot juste après parce qu'il a consommé son token de tempo pour faire cette rotation et il ne peut pas reset. Prendre une wave
qui n'est pas supposée être à toi, c'est réduire les options de jeu des adversaires, voire même les annuler. On veut te dive
mais tu clear la wave avant qu'elle arrive sans qu'ils s'y attendent... Eh bien bravo, tu as annulé leur tempo. Ici le Lucian cheat la wave, ce qui force les adversaires
à push sur seulement une lane, voire carrément de devoir attendre
la prochaine wave mid pour siège les T3. Tempo perdue : Eh oui, quand tu greed une wave en side ou que tu farm des loups pour terminer un item mais qu'on est à 15sec de la Soul, que t'es clairement en retard à cause de ça et que ta team ne peut pas temporiser ton arrivée, tu ruines totalement la tempo qui te permettrait de catch
ou de jouer autour du dragon. Ne faites pas ça. Tuer ton adversaire c'est génial mais greed une plate ou deux
dans les mauvais timings c'est ignoble parce que soit tu vas avoir un horrible back après, ou soit tu vas carrément mourir, mais dans les deux cas,
tout ce que tu as gagné en plate, ton adversaire va récupérer
en double pendant ton absence et ça marche plus tard aussi
pour les T2 et sur la carte en général. Attention à vos recall timer. Quand ta compo dépend d'un ulti pour engage ou bloquer celle de l'adversaire, le louper, c'est peine capitale pour ta team. Il faut donc lâcher la position,
attendre le retour du CD avec du crossmap ou en respectant et réinvestir la carte à ce moment-là. Si tu décides de contester tout de même, voilà ce qui arrivera. Avoir la tempo et l'utiliser,
c'est aussi la donner à l'adversaire. Bah oui, parce que ici,
après avoir gagné un fight, l'équipe gagnante utilise son token de tempo pour faire le dragon mais le temps de le faire
donne l'occasion aux adversaires de respawn ensemble en premier et de prendre la position à l'opposé. Ils récupèrent
donc naturellement la tempo et l'utilisent. Et c'est pas la Karma qui conteste toute seule
qui va y changer grand chose. Cheat la tempo : Parce que oui, ne pas avoir la tempo et vouloir la récupérer, c'est très souvent synonyme de devoir sacrifier une de tes ressources, que ce soit une wave,
un camp ou même une tour pour pouvoir jouer au jeu. C'est un gamble. Et regardons justement certaines de ces méthodes. Skip une wave,
c'est la base du gameplay assassin, et c'est ce qui te permet d'avoir de la tempo en brûlant une wave justement. C'est un gamble à double tranchant, si ça passe pas,
tu seras encore plus derrière, mais parfois c'est la seule option pour comeback ou pour snowball, surtout en mid late, via les sides. Et quand tu affrontes des champions qui tirent plus vite que toi
comme Zyra ou Zed en jungle, une solution pour avoir la tempo de faire une action
sans risque de countergank, c'est de skip un camp, voire même deux, et ça marche VRAIMENT bien. Mais si tu n'y gagnes rien, tu donnes une énorme tempo à l'adversaire. T'as compris, ces exemples, c'est du gambling en boucle ! Mais c'est parfois la seule solution et aussi la MEILLEURE ! Et enfin, les adversaires ont la tempo ? Très bien, trap dans un buisson seul ou à plusieurs pendant qu'ils investissent la carte et voilà, t'as volé leur tempo
juste avec un gamble qui est passé. C'est pour ça que je vous dis toujours
d'investir la carte à plusieurs pour réagir à ce genre de trap
et d'éviter de perdre la TEMPO. C'était long hein ? Mais c'est bon,
la théorie c'est enfin fini ! T'as vu tout ce que j'avais
à t'apprendre sur la tempo. Enfin tout, dose hein ! Y a beaucoup plus à dire sur ce sujet mais après la vidéo va durer 40 minutes comme le guide Jungle Tracking
que t'as jamais fini... Et où t'as loupé un banger incroyable. Mais je t'en veux pas,
t'inquiète, je comprends. Pas facile de se concentrer
plus que quelques secondes... Bref, faisons quand même
un très rapide résumé de tout ce qu'on a vu. La tempo, c'est savoir où et quand agir. Et justement, la réussite de chacune de tes actions impacte et est impactée par la tempo. On a aussi vu qu'il y avait différents types de tempo mais le plus important à retenir, c'est de se synchroniser avec ses alliés pour avoir le maximum d'impact sur la carte. Ensuite, on a comparé la tempo à un token dans une salle d'arcade pour comprendre son utilisation et ses conséquences. Une très belle métaphore,
si tu veux mon avis. Et après, on a vu que chaque compo avait des besoins de tempos différents, que les waves étaient la clé derrière le concept et qu'il fallait faire très attention à bien les gérer, surtout à partir du mid game. Et enfin, je t'ai montré
un bon nombre d'exemples d'actions qui peuvent ruiner ta tempo, en gagner ou voler celle de l'adversaire. Bon, je t'ai pas tout montré évidemment, parce qu'il y en a tellement
que ce serait trop long. Voilà, ça c'est tout ce qu'on a fait. Par contre, ce qu'on n'a pas encore fait et qui est commune à toutes mes vidéos, c'est le niveau Challenger. Mais que dirais-tu de skip cette étape cette fois-ci, hein ? Pourquoi pas directement monter d'un cran ? Pourquoi pas analyser comment est utilisée la tempo dans des conditions de jeu réelles, au meilleur niveau international, par la meilleure équipe que le jeu ait connue, la seule équipe 5x Championne du Monde et détendeur du back-to-back-to-back... Les T1 ! Deux minutes ! Je vais t'analyser deux minutes de tempo entre deux équipes
qui se renvoient coup pour coup, à un BO3 de l'élimination des Worlds. Et cette fois-ci, tu n'as rien à faire, il n'y a pas d'exercice. C'est à toi de t'adapter comme tu peux, et de comprendre ce qui se passe en utilisant tout ce que tu connaissais déjà, et tout ce que tu as appris au fil de cette vidéo. T'as compris ? Alors, allons-y ! T1 contre MKOI. Deuxième game du BO,
le perdant est éliminé des Worlds. On est à 16 minutes de jeu en position de 1-3-1, à une minute de l'apparition du troisième dragon. T1 en a déjà deux et on commence avec Faker
qui force la TP out de Jojo, push et crash la wave pour taper la tour. Cependant, il va greed sa tempo pour la finir et ne pourra donc pas prendre la wave bot avant que KOI la push, ce qui force Doran à compenser son erreur en utilisant sa TP bot pour cheat la wave
avant que Jojo n'arrive. De plus, après la tour top, Faker n'a pas pris la wave canon, ce qui donne la tempo à Myrwn et sa Ambessa pour pressure avec sa TP advantage. C'est lui qui nique le jeu et c'est un gros problème. T1 réalise alors leur erreur et décide d'abandonner totalement
les waves et la tour top. Faker va open mid
pour avoir l'avantage numérique et start le drake pour forcer une réaction de Myrwn, tout ça en restant hors de vision pour surprendre si un fight s'enclenche. Cependant, alors qu'ils ont la tempo, MKOI décide de ne pas contester et de seulement prendre la tour top. Mais tout était prévu ! Le piège de KOI était de laisser T1 faire le dragon, push la wave mid pour forcer la bot lane T1 sous tour puis de courir bot en bloquant la rotation et l'accès jungle pour dive en 4v2 ! CEPENDANT ! Gumayusi a tout de suite compris le piège et va sans aucune hésitation cheat la wave mid alors qu'il était seul face à 4 joueurs MKOI, dont le hover Myrwn avec sa Ambessa. MKOI va tout de même continuer
leur plan de dive bot, mais comme ils n'ont pas réussi
à bloquer le passage, ce qui devait être un 4v2 se retrouve
être un 4v4 à cause de Guma ! MAIS CE N'EST PAS FINI ! Myrwn avait cheat mid
en amont depuis la top lane, ce qui permet à MKOI de rotate et de jouer la tour mid pendant que les T1 sont encore bloqués bot sans possibilité de rotate ! MAIS NON ! Faker est déjà là pour clear la wave et sauver la tour avant même
que tous les joueurs KOI n'arrivent ! Comment est-ce possible ? Parce que plutôt de continuer à push après avoir clear la wave au top, Faker avait déjà compris
et anticipé la seule réaction possible de ses adversaires
suite à leur échec du dive au bot, et il va donc rotate en amont pour défendre la tour au timing parfait, ce qui va neutraliser intégralement
et une bonne fois pour toutes tout le plan de jeu de KOI et relancer la game
sur une toute nouvelle tempo ! 2 MINUTES ! C'ÉTAIT SEULEMENT 2 MINUTES
DE TEMPO D'UNE PARTIE PARMIS DES MILLIONS DE JOUÉES
CHAQUE ANNÉE DANS LE MONDE ! 16 ANS QUE LE JEU EXISTE ! ET POURTANT CE N'EST QUE RÉCEMMENT
QU'ON A COMMENCÉ À VRAIMENT MAÎTRISER LE CONCEPT DE TEMPO ! MAIS ALORS LA QUESTION SE POSE ! AVONS-NOUS RÉELLEMENT DÉCOUVERT TOUT CE QUE LEAGUE OF LEGENDS À A NOUS OFFRIR ? LA COMPLEXITÉ DU JEU EST SANS FIN ! ALORS AVONS-NOUS VRAIMENT
ATTEINT LES LIMITES DU JEU ? OU ALORS EXISTE-T-IL
UN CONCEPT INCONNU DE TOUS QUI N'ATTEND QUE D'ÊTRE DÉCOUVERT ? Et ça, c'est à toi de me le dire ! Mais attention, pas besoin de s'emballer pour si peu voyons, League of Legends ne reste
qu'un simple jeu vidéo après tout. Un jeu qui peut parfois tirer de sacrées émotions de nous,
je te l'accorde. En tout cas, j'espère que
c'était ton cas en regardant cette vidéo. Merci à toi de l'avoir regardé jusqu'au bout, et à toutes les personnes qui s'affichent à l'écran,
(COUCOU C'EST MOI, JE PASSE A LA TV MAMAN) qui m'ont tous aidé d'une façon ou d'une autre. Et pars pas tout de suite ! Si t'as aimé la vidéo,
n'oublie pas de mettre un like et de commenter n'importe quoi ! Et si t'es sur téléphone, lâche un boost ! Qui sait, peut-être un jour
le million de vues sur ma chaîne ! Allez, ciao bye-bye !

titre: BECOME THE PERFECT SUPPORT! League of Legends Guide
transcription: bienvenue dans ce guide support aujourd'hui on va parler de toutes les notions dont tu as besoin un bon support je ferai au fur à mesure tous les autres rôles alors abonne-toi pour ne rien rater la vidéo est structurée du plus basique aux notions importantes à plus haut niveau installez-vous bien on va pouvoir commencer les types de support il existe trois types de support les tanks les tanks vont avoir beaucoup de CC et peu de dégâts ils pourront rester devant leur carry et et prendre les sorts à leur place pour ne pas qu'il meurent les support tank s'associent bien avec tout type d'ADC les enchanteurs les enchanteurs vont booster une personne et vont mieux que ça soit un carry parce que donner du SHIELD à un malfit c'est drôle mais ça ne va pas vous faire gagner la partie les enchanteurs restent un peu plus en retrait et s'associe très bien avec les hyper caris les mages les mages vont faire des dégâts en Contin continu ce qu'on appelle le POC c'est le fait de gratter petit à petit les HP de notre adversaire les mages ont aussi la possibilité de burst burst sait mettre beaucoup de dégâts en un instant il s'associe bien avec des ADC qui mettent de la pression sur la l et qui sont agressifs en début de partie vous devez donc essayer d'adapter un maximum votre pic avec votre équipe si votre ADC prend un hyper carry privilégier un enchanteur ou un tank s'il prend un champion agressif prenez de l'engage ou un mage si vous ne savez pas quel combo est fort n'hésitez pas à aller voir sur internet pas mal de sites vous donneront les meilleurs duo line on va parler rapidement des skill shot comme les cages ou bien les grabes un support est dangereux quand il peut vous Grabe et seulement quand il l' à son Grabe alors soyez patient lorsque vous jouez support balancer des sorts au hasard sera contre productif je dédierai une vidéo spécialement au skill shot dans un futur proche alors restez connectés ça arrive fort parlons maintenant du deuxième point de cette vidéo le 2 versus 2 en tant que support placez-vous vers le bas de la Line et jouez avec les buissons prenez le contrôle du Buisson du milieu avec ce dernier vous mettez plus de pression sur les adversaires et vous pouvez surprendre plus facilement en balançant vos sorts directement du buisson pour défendre ce buisson si vous êtes contre ce cas de figure il faut tout simplement placer une vision dedans pour ne pas se faire surprendre votre rôle en tant que support est de trouver des engag ou des trades sur laadc en face focalisez-vous sur l'élasti qu'il va devoir faire et synchronisez vos sorts là-dessus quand un champion autoattaque il devient alors immobile donc plus facile à toucher voici un exemple vidéo je vais rester volontairement dans la range de vein pour qu'elle me tape gratuitement entre guillemets car la tentation de faire proc sa marque est trop grande elle va revenir mettre une auto et c'est à ce moment-là qu'elle est vulnérable donc frequill en principe vous devez focus laadc mais dans certains cas le support sera plus intéressant cela dépend surtout de la facilité à le tuer et à son positionnement et pour comprendre ça il faut connaître le principe du positionnement en triangle lorsque vous jouez au bot vous êtes deux donc votre positionnement doit s'adapter à celui des deux adversaires en prenant en compte la distance entre chaque champion parfois un triangle se forme et fait en sorte que vous avez l'avantage ou non c'est peut-être un fait du hasard ou bien on peut faire en sorte que ça soit volontaire pour éviter cette contrer ce principe la solution est la même restez aligné il vous suffit désormais de vous coordonner par rapport au sorts de votre ADC s'il vient d'envoyer ses sorts les plus importants ne tentez pas de Goï il ne pourra pas vous suivre correctement il y a encore bien d'autres choses à prendre en compte mais pour le devv2 on continuera plus tard on va passer à un nouveau point super important pour les supports la vision le support est celui qui doit gérer le plus la vision le premier setup de Ward qu'on verra est le setup en face de Line essayez de toujours garder votre Ward pour votre phase de Line ou au moins une en comptant celle de la DC la première WE à placer sera après 2 minutes 30 à environ 3 minutes de jeu c'est le timing de gang des jungleers donc prenez du temps pour warder un buisson pour voir le gang arriver alors oui mais on warde où nous le choix du Buisson se décide selon l'état de votre line si vous pchez et vous êtes avancé sur la line Wez plutôt le deuxème buisson et le 3è si vous comptez aller mettre la pression sous la tour placez votre war dans le premier ou le deuxième buisson si vous êtes au milieu de la laine si un jungler ennemi peut passer au-dessus des murs comme Chaco privilégie le premier buisson car il peut passer par-dessus le pil de dragon pour esquiver la vision et pour le dernier quand vous êtes sous votre tour vous pensez que c'est le premier buisson et ben vous avez pas totalement raison parce que si vous êtes sous tour comment vous allez mettre cette Ward sans vous faire taper par les adversaires je vous ai vu là bon la solution est de soit warder avant que la vague arrive sous tour ou soit de faire le tour pour poser la warde faites attention si les adversaires sortent de votre vision ils peuvent vous attendre dans le buisson en espérant que vous allez warder la deuxième solution c'est tout simplement de warder juste à côté par-dessus le mur la vision n'est pas parfaite mais au moins vous ne prenez pas de risque et vous saurez si vous allez vous faire dive ou inveille dans la jungle la Ward c'est fait maintenant il y a aussi la Pink la balise de contrôle en tant que support c'est super important de toujours en avoir une sur vous mais vous n'êtes pas obligé de la mettre à chaque fois si vous êtes trop sous pression sous votre tour mettre une pink ne fera que donner des gold gratuitement aux adversaires et dans le sens inverse mettre une pink très agressif alors que vous ne pouvez pas la défendre reviendra au même problème on place la Pink derrière la Ward pour savoir si des ennemis se rapprochent et peut-être pouvoir la défendre s'il souhaitent la retirer si vous Gez le bush numéro 2 pinkz le bush numéro 1 si vous Gez le bush numéro 3 pinkz le bush numéro 2 vous avez compris le principe on met toujours la Pink derrière la Ward parlons d'un cas particulier qui peut vous sauver la vie les les ennemis préparent un slow push et s'apprête à Bac en tout cas sur le papier car s'ils veulent ils peuvent se cacher dans le buisson en faisant semblant d'avoir bac pour vous tuer quand vous allez push ça ne vous est pas déjà arrivé pour la contrée il suffit de warder un buisson avant que le slow push n'avance trop loin er le deuxième buisson pour avoir toute la vision sur les buissons aux alentour ou bien le premier si vous voulez être sûr qu'ils vont back on peut aussi warder le dernier si l'un de vos alliés souhaite se téléporter sur votre line si vous n'avez pas de vision essayez juste de balancer des sorts quand vous allez warder vous allez devoir quitter votre line alors quelle est go any further we first have to talk about DPI DPI stands for Dots per inch and to put it super super simply it's basically just how quickly your cursor moves relative to how much you're moving the
