titre: 10 BEGINNER Tips & Tricks You MUST KNOW - Wild Rift (LoL Mobile)
transcription: [Music] what's going on pro guides fam and welcome back to another wild rift video my name is kangas and today we're going to be giving you 10 tips that'll help you jump into wild rift with ease the goal is to create a solid foundation for learning so try to apply what you can from this video in your games now let's get into it tip number one try out as many champs as you can when you start playing wild rift you definitely won't know what every champion does unless you've played league before you should try out a bunch of different champions so you can learn about the differences between roles and play styles these playstyles can vary from tanky frontliners like malphite to glass cannon hypercarries like vayne a good rule of thumb is to start with an easier champion like garen and malphite before you move your way up to the more mechanically challenging ones like vayne and camille once you find out a few champions that you enjoy stick with them the best way to get better at the game starts with your first main don't worry about tier lists or opinions any champion can reach any rank pick who you like and take time to learn their strengths and weaknesses number two learn the language if you're new to the league of legends universe you might not know everything that players are saying there's a ton of lingo that comes with playing this game and it'll take you some time to learn most players who play wild rift will have come from league of legends or another moba game and might assume that everyone they're playing with understands these terms if you're a brand new player we released a complete beginner's guide to wild rift where you can go through terminology and other useful information that you'll need to know communication is extremely valuable in wild rift so making sure you're up to speed is also super important tip number three stay behind your minions during the laning phase it's important to stand with your friendly minions whenever you can if you stand outside of them you can open yourself up to being easily hit by skill shots and taking free damage when you're inside the safety of your minion wave try to focus your attention on last hitting when you can but if the enemy is looking to attack you remember to always trade back never take damage for free you can also use this concept against your opponents when your minions are getting low look to trade with the enemy laner they'll have to pick between trading back with you or losing the minion gold a lose-lose scenario if you see that your opponent just used their abilities to hit the minion wave you can step up and attack them they won't have any way to fight back easily on top of this every time a champion auto attacks they're locked in an animation use that time to effectively trade with the enemy laner understanding how minions affect laning phase is really important and can be the difference between you starting a lane kingdom or pinging in your jungler for help the next tip is learn the map when you start out playing wild rift it's easy to just walk into one of the three lanes baron mid and dragon line but what do you do after you've taken your turret how do you position yourself to take dragon understanding higher level concepts things like jungle timers and vision are incredibly important when it comes to learning the game as a laner don't be afraid to roam around a bit and interact with the other champions in the game the support and mid-roll are especially good at roaming so give those a try so you can experience the skirmish in the river it might seem a bit scary to jungle but doing so will really help you learn about clearing camps and jungle pathing there's a lot more to wild rift than just fighting champions in lane so get out there and give it a try yourself tip number five set up your loadout before you queue up before you load into a game it's important to have a general idea of what the best items and runes are for your chosen champion most champs have an optimal rune setup and items are often fairly straightforward there are youtube channels like pro guides that can tell you the best builds and loadouts for each champ making sure to have these set up for yourself will increase your chances of winning before you even load into the rift once you get further into your wild rift journey you'll know exactly what runes you want for each matchup and game for now try to use runes that others have success with the recommended runes are a decent place to start if you want to focus on just learning the game first tip number six communicate with your team it can be hard to guess what your teammates want to do and the outcome of wild rift games are heavily decided on teamwork so communication is key a great way to easily communicate with your team is through the ping function it's a good habit to try and let your team know when you're coming to gank or when you want to engage or disengage if you're struggling to get on the same page with just pings chat can help get your team in sync if you're not sure what to do in the game ask your team what the win condition is if a teammate seems lost offer them a helping hand teamwork makes the dream work tip number seven make sure to use your wards the number one mistake i see from newer players is that they underestimate the importance of warding in wild rift since most of the map is obscured by fog of war knowledge is power there are three major ways to gain vision to make better decisions first ward in your lane bush so the enemy laner can't duck out a vision you can also ward the enemy jungle camp so you can track the enemy jungler the most important tip for beginners is toward the river so you know if you're going to get ganked remember wards are free don't let them just sit in your inventory drop them i promise you they'll level up your gameplay tip number eight mute those toxic players the league of legends community is notorious for having a good amount of toxic players and trolls so don't feel bad when you use the mute button the chat is a tool to help you win more games but if what's being said isn't helping you win make sure to drop a mute on them and just move on these players will often flood the chat with hate so ignore them and keep playing the game keep trying your best and if it seems doomed focus on yourself and don't fall into the habit of typing back tip number nine round out your team comp in wild rift you'll be team fighting often so having a balanced team composition is vital to coming out ahead every team needs a balance of tankiness damage crowd control and wave clear the first step is to think about what champions your team has picked and what champions would complement those picks does your team have lots of damage but no engage pick malphite do you need wave clear luck and orianna enemy team has a bunch of assassins janna is the choice laning matchups are important in wild rift to win the early game but the team with the better composition will usually emerge victorious if you're looking to just insta-lock your champion for practice you can't always expect your team to build around you if you're trying your best to win the game talk with your team about what kind of picks to play together tip number 10 help your teammates out every time you queue up for a game alone it's rolling the dice you're matched with four random teammates and sometimes all of your teammates will stomp their lanes and carry you to a smooth victory sometimes they'll relentlessly feed and make the game impossible it's totally normal to have games like this so just do your best to help them out when you can if you can secure a lead it's important to push your lane and roam around the map to help out your team the most effective way to win the game isn't to try and solo push 1v5 it's to win the entire map even one kill can completely turn around a losing matchup so try to make the most out of the windows that you have to roam also if your laner can roam make sure to ping your teammates back this will let your team focus on their own gameplay with the trust that you'll let them know if they're in trouble now these tips definitely aren't everything you need to know to become a fierce competitor on the rift but they'll give you a major head start wild rift has an incredible level of depth in every area of the game so don't feel too frustrated if it doesn't all come natural immediately it'll come with time that's it for the video thanks for watching let us know in the comments below how your climb is going and any tips you think we should have included don't forget to subscribe if you want more wild rift guides and updates and of course best of luck on the rift [Music] [Music] [Applause] [Music] you

titre: 3 Minute Kayn Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Kane jungle? Well, he is amazingly mobile around the map. He is a very versatile pick. He deals massive damage to either tanks or squishies, and he's one of the best objective stealers in the game. However, he is weaker before getting his form, and he is damage reliant, so can struggle from behind. Kane's passive is he gains experience towards turning into his red form or blue form. Every time he damages an enemy champion or gets a takedown, he gets orbs, which grants experience towards it. When he damages melee enemies, he gets orbs towards his red form. And when he damages ranged enemies, he gets orbs towards his blue form. Once you have enough, when you go back to your fountain, you can transform. However, if you get the form that you don't want, after four minutes, you can choose the other. Red Kane gets a passive where he heals for damage he deals to enemy champions with abilities. Blue Kane gets a passive where for the first 3 seconds in combat, he deals bonus damage. Kane's Q has two parts to it. The first is a straight line dash, which deals damage to enemies he passes through, and then he deals damage in a circle around him. If he's transformed into Red Kane, this now deals great max health damage. Kane's W is a rectangular skill shot which deals damage to enemies hit and slows them. If he's in red form, this also knocks them up. And if he's in blue form, the range is extended and he can move after using it. Kane's E grants him bonus movement speed and the ability to move through terrain. He heals himself when moving inside. And as long as he doesn't stay out of the terrain or get damaged or CCd, he can stay inside for a long time. And while it's Blue Kane, the cool down is lowered, you gain 100% slow resist, and the bonus move speed is increased. Kane's ultimate can only be used against an enemy he's recently damaged. It causes him to vanish and dash inside them where after 2 seconds he dashes out of them dealing damage. Or he can recast it early to dash out of them sooner. While in blue form, the cast range and emerge range is increased. And emerging will reset his extra damage passive. And while in red form, he deals massive max health damage and heals himself. For combos, your basic go-to is to attack into Q, attack into W, then attack into alt. And when engaging, use E into W, and attack. From here, Q into an attack before using alt. For matchups, Red Kane is great into tanky junglers, but can struggle into jewelists. And Blue Kane is great at assassinating squishy junglers, but can struggle against those he can't assassinate. For runes, Red Kane goes conqueror, and Blue Kane goes dark harvest. For build order, Red Kane builds a bruiser build, which looks like this, and Blue Kane builds an assassin build, which looks like this. For skill order, start Q, then E, then W before maxing Q, then W, then E, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and smite. Starting the game, most junglers are trying to get golden experience, but you're also heavily focused on getting orbs to evolve to your form as fast as possible. You normally want to start raptors and then do a full jungle clear. From here, you do want to be ganking. Look to gank range champions if you want to be blue Kane and mele champions if you want to be red. Your E gives you very unique gank path, so be creative when going for the gank. And even though you're not the strongest at ganking early, it's all about getting them orbs. Entering the mid game, play a pick and objective play style. Look to run through terrain, get kills, and convert into objectives. And you're also one of the best objective stealers in the game. So either run through terrain and steal it or ult onto the enemy jungler and steal it while you're unargetable. Entering late game team fight as blue cane, you're the assassin. Wait for the fight to start then E through terrain onto an enemy carry. as red cane play front to back where you can easily kill tanks then dive the backline.

titre: How To Enable Streamer Mode In League of Legends (Quick Tutorial)
transcription: Hey everyone. In this quick tutorial, I'll show you how to turn on streamer mode in League of Legends. It's super simple and takes less than a minute. First, open your League of Legends client and click on the settings icon. Scroll down until you find the option called streamer mode. As you can see, it's already enabled for me, but you can easily turn it on or off right here. And that's it. You've successfully enabled streamer mode in League of Legends. If this video helped you out, make sure to like and subscribe for more quick tutorials. See you next time.

titre: League of Legends Best Settings & Hotkeys (Beginner Friendly)
transcription: in this video I'll reveal the best League of Legends settings to boost your game performance giving you a significant Edge in every match so let's jump straight into it and load up League of Legends and you want to come to the settings menu in the top right here and the first thing we're going to do is head into the hotkeys Tab and the first setting we're going to change is this quick cast setting now once you've got used to the basics of League of Legends you should no longer use the normal cast as the normal cast requires you to do an extra click to use the Ability which slows down the process so by using this quick cast option it gives you the opportunity to use your abilities faster so in order to turn this on WE simply need to hit the quick cast all button here and then in terms of the actual hot Keys these are all pretty standard and it's what most of the pro players use so when we scroll down you'll see a section where it says quick cast with indicator this is down to personal preference but if you want the quickest spellcast like most of the pro players then I'd recommend turning this option off we can then come into the interface section and the first thing we're going to change here is this HUD scale as it's super important to be able to see as much of the map as possible so because League massively rewards Vision we need to reduce this HUD to be able to see more of the screen so just move the slider all the way to zero for the chat scale it's a similar situation you want to keep this between 50 to 70 the mini map scale we want to keep at maximum as map awareness is definitely one of the most important fundamental skills to learn in the game so the bigger your map is the easier it's going to be to see object planning we can just leave this one as default we want to obviously show health bars and where it says show names above the health bar we want to set this to Champion name again this is down to a personal preference but Champion name is the most common selection here down in the ability intact display option we want to select show spell costs as selecting this option will help you keep track of your Mana without the need to hover over them to see how many maners you have left we then want to come into the game Tab and first thing you'll see is your mouse options now it's better to actually leave these as default and if you want to speed up or slow down your mouse sensitivity it's better to do this in your actual Mouse settings on your computer however there are a couple of top players that change the set settings in here I don't see any real need to do it inside of League when you have the option to do it on your Windows computer but again this one comes down to personal preference and important one to turn off here is auto attack as there's really no benefit to having this selected and again most of the pro players keep this one off and then we can save these changes and we're just going to change a few graphic settings which we need to do inside of a custom game so once you're inside of a game just press the Escape key to bring back up the settings menu then inside of here we're going to come over to video now these settings are based on the player Faker are really popular A League of Legends player and in order to replicate his settings you want to keep the character quality to high environment quality should be set to high effects quality can be set to high and then Shadow quality we want to set to low we want to disable this enable screen shake option and then we can just press okay to save our changes and that's about it guys just by changing those few settings that should now improve your gameplay hopefully this video helped you and if you did like it please drop a like And subscribe until next time

titre: How to learn unlocked camera in lol [GUIDE]
transcription: in this video I'm going to be teaching you guys how to master unlock camera in a few simple steps let's get straight into it so boom first thing I want you to do is go to hot Keys camera control toggle camera log unbind that [ __ ] now the next step is actually your choice but a lot of players going to have their camera set to fixed offset over per side offset per side offset just makes it kind of not dead center it like starts from your side so if you start red side the camera will kind of look like this in a way instead of like dead center this is not even dead center but this is dead center so yeah in a way that kind of makes me more comfortable with moving the screen cuz usually when I play with pide it makes me feel like I got to move kind of diagonally a lot versus like top bottom left right and lastly you got your space bar use the space bar mind you I'm a victim of lock camera back when I played I would literally turn on or turn off the lock pretty much I just press y y y instead of just pressing space bar so use the space bar to your advantage definitely um make it a muscle memory of yours and you'll actually Master unlock camera and I know there are plenty of times where you actually lose your champion in the middle of a fight use the space bar

titre: HOW TO PLAY NEEKO IN 2025 | League of Legends
transcription: [Music] So, you want to learn how to play Nico? Well, you've come to the right place. My name is Vegas. I'm a diamond Nico one trick who plays are in the mid uh jungle and support roles. And today, I'm going to teach you the the basics of playing Nico, as well as some fun tips and tricks. Now, this is more of a beginners's video just to go over her abilities and some fun um you know, tricks, strats that I use. If you want like a full in-depth like uh commentary guide on a game, I've got plenty of other videos like that on my channel. So, go check them out. But, let's get started. Now, Nico's passive, Inherent Glamour. This allows her to disguise herself as allied champions using this bar at the the bottom here above your items. And as you can see, it's filled with Cho'Gaf, Twitch, Alistister, and Trindir. These are the champs that I've got in this game. And I can transform into them by just clicking like that. But additionally, instead of clicking, you can actually keybind it and you can switch a lot quicker. How do you keybind it? Go to your hotkeys. Scroll down abilities and summoner spells. Scroll down to other. And where it says champion specific interaction uh one through five, that's what all of those are. And it just allows you to like just change so much quicker than if you were to like click them. There's also an empty uh slot here. And that is for transforming into things like minions. You can also change into jungle camps like the scuttle crab. Now this works for blue buff which you can dance with grump raptors wolves. You can change into any jungle monster apart from the epic ones such as dragon, baron, rift herald. You can also change into the tiny like the little grubs. It doesn't stop there. You can also transform into wards. You can also change into jungle plants. And not only that, you can transform into champion specific traps and pets such as like a Nefiri dog or a Yoro ghoul, a Teemo Shroom. You can transfer transform into all of these um sho boxes and it can really throw the enemy offic. Now, how do you lose the transformation? If you use a damaging ability such as E, Q, or your ultimate, that will transform you back into Nico. If I was to transform into a minion and then attack a tower, that will transform me back into Nico. However, if I transform into an allied champion and attack a tower, I will stay as the allied champion. The only other thing that will break a transformation is if I transform to an allied champion with extremely low health and I take a bit of damage or if I get CCD in any sort of way like any route stun, knockup, suppression, um, displacement, all of that will transform me back into Nico. Next, let's talk about Nico's Q, Blooming Burst. Nico froze. I see that blooms to deal magic damage. If it kills a unit or hits a champion or large monster, it will bloom again, dealing magic damage. Max two extra blooms. So, if you just throw it on nothing, this is what it looks like. This is the range and the area of effect size. And if you throw it on an enemy champion, it will bloom up to two extra times, but only if they um remain within the bloom. So if it hits a enemy champion and they move out, it'll only bloom like one extra time. Now, this is great for a number of things apart from dealing damage. You can see there's an enemy in this bush. But let's say we were scouting this bush and we didn't know if someone was in there. Just throwing our Q and seeing the bloom lets us know, okay, there's an enemy in this bush. And if you to throw it in a bush without an enemy, you can, you know, it's safe. But it's also good for clearing minion waves. We see a minion with low health. Okay, I've got too much AP. There we go. If it kills a minion, it will bloom again. And you can use this to like clear your minion waves. Just like that. Now, before I talk about Nico's W, I'm actually going to talk about her E, Tangle Barbs. Now, her E is a route. You throw it like that, it will root your enemy. At level two, it's.9 seconds. other one's.7. But if your route hits something else, like a minion or another champion, it will become empowered, rooting the second target for twice as long. And you can see the projectile moves quicker, gets wider, and roots for longer. Alors, je sais que eux sont au stade final et le but c'est le chemin, mais on te demande pas d'avoir 90, on te demande d'avoir 55 et 55 c'est juste par exemple gagner ta line, avoir en fait avoir plus d'impact que le liner qui est en face de toi ou le jungler qui est en face de toi tout simplement. Et une fois que tu as compris ça, bah en fait la technique c'est très simple. Tu prends un OTP, tu le spammes à la limite deux champions, tu les spammes, tu fais le plus de games dans ce champion là, tu le maîtrises dans ses combos, tu vois en entraînement, tu regardes des guides, tu te remets en question sur ce champion, tu essayes de comprendre véritablement. C'est-à-dire que tu vas te dire "OK, dans ce matchup, il fait ces runes là. Dans ce matchup, il fait ça. Pourquoi ? OK, c'est intéressant." Et une fois que tu as fait ça, je te jure que tu vas progresser à une vitesse fulgurante parce que tu vas enfin te focus sur les aspects qui font que tu montes véritablement sur le jeu. C'est gagner sa lane, bien snowball, bien rotate, bien impacter son équipe. Comment faire pour voir plus de win rate ? Et tu verras, ça ira crendo tout simplement. Donc voilà, je vous avais envie de vous faire cette vidéo, ça me tenait vraiment à cœur parce que moi personnellement, ça a été mon cas. J'ai OTP CLED, j'étais bronze, j'étais littéralement un bronze, je suis monté gold et après en une année quand j'ai commencé à vraiment bien spammer le jeu parce que bah je streamais et cetera, n'hésitez pas à venir d'ailleurs sur mon stream, bah j'ai fait Gold to Master en une saison. Après, il y avait pas l'émerode mais voilà, c'est quand même Gold to Master, c'est une véritable perf et je suis pas plus intelligent que vous. Je suis juste un mec qui a essayé d'optimiser son apprentissage à me dire "OK, il faut que je connaisse tous mes matchups parfaitement, il faut que je connaisse tous mes combos parfaitement, comment le jouer parfaitement le led game et cetera et ça a marché." Et regardez aujourd'hui, j'ai atteint le GM et peut-être que l'année prochaine je serai challenger ou pas. Mais voilà, en tout cas, j'avais envie de vous faire cette vidéo pour vous expliquer cela. J'espère qu'elle vous a plu. N'hésitez pas à commenter, à partager et à liker cette vidéo. Portez-vous bien. C'était. Ciao. Ciao.

titre: How to play ARAM Mayhem (How do augments work?) - League of Legends
transcription: This video is going to dive into a Mayhem and what it is and how to play it. We're going to touch on three different subjects [music] being champ select screen, the augments and set bonuses you'll get throughout the game, as well as stat shards. Let's get right into it. All right, we can get right into it. I'll leave timestamps below and on the screen right now showing the different sections of the video so you can either skip around or stay for the whole info dump. [music] Okay, so champ select. To begin, we can start with the word aim. This is actually an acronym standing for all random, all mid. So, how that impacts champ select is that each player will be shown two champions they can choose from. After one is chosen, the others will go into a pool for all of your team to select from, allowing you a random pool of champions to play. There's also a small chance to receive a blessed card, granting you a third champion to choose from. There's a 5% chance of this happening plus an additional 0.15% for each champion you own. This means if you have all 172 champions, you have 30.8% chance of pulling the third bless card. [music] Augments are where Abraham Mayhem really gets interesting. Augments will be awarded to players at level 3, 7, 11, and 15, being awarded once you die. This results in a total of four augments throughout the game. There are only three different rarities of augment going from best to worst. There's prismatic, gold, and silver. Everyone will receive the same rarity of augments given at each different level. So, if you get a prismatic at level seven, then everyone else will also be receiving a prismatic when they get to level seven. Attached to some augments in the lefth hand corner of the card are set bonuses. These grant you extra buffs on top of the augment for collecting multiple augments in that set. You can see what the set bonus does and each of the other augments required to complete it by hovering over the mini set icon. Each set gives an extra bonus that would help those specific augments. For example, the bonus stacksurus rex consists of nine different stacking augments including infinite recursion, master of duality, phenomenal evil, quest steal your heart, shrink engine, slapound, soul eater, tap dancer, and upgrade hubris. This set grants more stacks for each augment you acquire in the stackas for aurora set. So if you get two augments of this set, you get a 50% increase in the stacks gained. But if you manage to find all four, you'll get a 200% [music] more stack bonus. So acquiring these full sets can be extremely beneficial, allowing for some insane stat bonuses. As of patch 26.3, there's a total of nine different set bonuses and 201 augments, which allows for quite a variety of games. [music] Stash shards are something new you'll be able to purchase from the shop after hitting level 9 for 750 gold per shard. Stat shards grant you three choices of random stats at varying amounts. You can be given the choices of something like health, lethality, or armor, and then you choose one and are granted those stats. Unlike in arena, stat shards are actually lost upon death. There's a small chance to get a prismatic stat shard, granting much higher stats in the realm of straight up 17.5% magic pen or something like 15% more health. These stats work off percentages rather than straight numbers allowing to really impact your build, especially if you get them late game. Since if you have 10,000 HP, 15% would grant you another 1500 HP versus if you only had 1,000 HP, that 15% [music] would only give you an extra 150. So, you really want to pick these up later. They're very strong. If you're looking to see some AAM mayhem gameplay before you step into the arena, you can check out some of my videos down in the description. And if you enjoyed this, then [music] make sure to hit the little subscribe button. It would do me a lot of favors. And I will see you in the next one. [music] >> [music]

titre: 3 Minute Sylas Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: why play silus mid well he is incredibly mobile with three dashes he brings tons of burst damage he's versatile being able to steal different ultimates and he scales very well with cooldown reduction however he is very skill shot reliant on his q and E and he can be Mana hungry in the early game silus is passive is after using an ability he generates a stack and he can have up to three stacks at one time these Stacks cause silas's next basic attack to have a large amount of increased attack speed and deal a large amount of damage in a circle silas's Q throws out two chains which converge at a point enemies hit take damage and are slowed then after a short delay where the chains crossed over explodes dealing damage again silas's W is a point and click ability which causes him to dash to the enemy and deal damage to them and if this was used against an enemy Champion silus also gets a large heal silas's e has two activations the first causes him to dash a short distance the second is a straight line skill shot which deals damage to and stuns the first Target Hit Upon hitting that Target silus then dashes to them and knocks them up silas's ultimate is one of the most versatile abilities in the game it is a point and click ability which allows him to steal an enemy Champion's ultimate he stores it for up to 90 seconds where he can use it and where the cool down of this ability isn't very long each enemy Champion also has their own cool down so you're encouraged to steal multiple different ultimates for combos a good go-to is use e into W and attack from here follow up with your second e into an attack into q and then spam out the rest of your attacks for matchups silus is great into longer range Champions easily dashing onto them dodging their skill shots and bringing them down however he can strug struggling to Champions who can keep him locked down for runes take this he procs conqueror incredibly easily for build order start ring and pot into a rocket belt and Tier Two Boots after this a cosmic drive and zos are great pickups before finishing a build with any of these as needed for skill order start e then W then Q before maxing W then e q taking alt whenever you can for Summoner spells take Flash and teleport starting the game you're looking to farm hard and take good trades your levels one and two are absolutely fine but your level three when you have access to all of your abilities is amazing your your ability to e towards your enemy and then W onto them gives you a massive zone of threat and as you're so incredibly mobile you can more or less choose to engage whenever you want to unless your opponent is playing so far away they're basically zoning themselves from the minions Landing a full combo at earliest level three is ridiculously good damage as using your full combo where you hit everything will give you four passive procs along with all your abilities damage you don't push the lane fast however this can work in stylus's favor as the further they are away from their Tower the more room you have to run them down and when you hit Level being able to steal their ultimate will usually give you a large amount of kill threat entering the mid game your Bot Lane will come mid which puts you in a side lane simply push it out as far as you can safely than group back up with your team for fights and objectives entering late game team fights it's all about stealing a good ultimate anything big in AOE is usually a good go-to but if they have a specific threat getting something to bring them down can be a good choice too otherwise play the Assassin role where you lur off at the side of the fight until it starts you rarely want to be the one going in and you want to wait for your opportunity to jump on an enemy backliner and remove them that

titre: BEST WASD Settings for League of Legends (High Elo ADC Guide)
transcription: Okay, I'm going to go over a settings video for WASD. So, obviously you want w a s d. And you can set stuff to q e r f at block tab shift control and see the overlay I have on the stream. Um so the way I like to do it is having your middle finger ring finger and index finger for your movement. But obviously if you want to hit q you're not going to hit q with your pinky. So, it's got to be something that you're going to quickly tap rarely. So, for me it's the target champions only. You can see the red gem inside the cursor turning on and off. Right there. Um cuz you're only going to turn that on you know once at the start of a fight. Quickly release your WASD for that or like your ultimate can stay on r if you want since you'll only press it once per fight. But I don't recommend putting like your q on q. But anyway So, my settings are all set up to mouse buttons. So, I have WASD for movement and then I have alt Right now you can't see that I'm holding alt but not on my overlay but hold alt to level up. Level up. Target champions only. And then alt plus mouse button alt plus mouse button. You see the little things lighting up. And middle mouse wheel for my ultimate. Right. So, this button is my main spam button. Then side mouse buttons for abilities and then middle mouse button for the ultimate. That way I can always keep WASD while doing my attacks. Um and then same reason for caps lock and shift since they're not like something you're going to be pressing a lot. You can put those on r f if you wanted to. I have shop on g but I thought about moving it to f but the issue is if I fat finger when I'm trying to move right. I put it on g. That's another issue you have if you put flash on f. When you're moving to the right you might fat finger your flash. But I put them on tab. Or sorry on my pinky. And my pinky hits tab to see the score. Hits tab to caps lock tab to flat or a caps lock to barrier shift to flash. And then control is used for um this one of the aiming ability. My this is where you set the the cast for indicator. I don't use it very much. I use it a lot more when I did um the old way click to move. I'll use like aiming Kaiser W's for example. Yeah, I haven't really should probably try to get into the habit of pressing it more. Oh right, I have r set up to cancel. So, what cancel would be like if you Let's say if you wanted to press this but then you decided you don't want to. Press r and it doesn't work. Oh, it does. I see. You have to release control. But yeah, there you go. Interesting. You can hold the spell that way. Uh two three and four set up to not quick cast. So, that when I play support I put wards on number two. And then this way you can see when your ward's going to be in the bush cuz it'll have the green circle around the cursor. Right. See, it'll just do it. But the rest are all on quick cast. Yeah, and then I have camera control for my teammates. Is zxcv. If I was clicking I wanted to see what they were doing. Uh movement cursor to blind so when I flash if I'm this I flash to the left even though my cursor's to the right. I've had this bug out a couple times like when I'm stunned or something. I'm holding right and spamming flash. It'll think that my character's not moving so I'll flash to my cursor. Like that. So, something to think about. I have color blind mode on to give me the golden health bar so it stands out more. For camera you shouldn't use a locked camera anymore. Uh free camera is definitely best but if you're not confident or comfortable bumping the camera around then you should use the dynamic camera. But there is really no reason to use the locked camera since dynamic is basically locked but slightly better. I like using fixed offset but for side offset it's fine. This makes it so when you center the camera your character is centered based on the side of the map you're on. Uh probably wouldn't recommend semi-locked where you kind of set the distance yourself. But if you like it go for it. And then in interface there's some important settings like you want show loss of control UI. It makes it so a little bar shows how long your cursor is stunned for or slowed for or whatever. Off-screen pings to help you see what's happening. Uh I would say disable heads-up display clicks so you don't accidentally click your flash. The WASD range indicator is really nice. Uh show spell costs is good so you can calculate if you have enough mana to do your combo. Show timestamps is really good. And you need to have this one on. Experience is off by default. But you should really have it on because it'll let you see I hit the wrong button. It'll let you see when you're in range. Cuz sometimes the lane phase will be really hard. And if that's the case then you might be playing on the very edge of your exp range. But the fastest way to learn what that range is is to have this little thing popping up. So, you'll know if these guys will die and I won't get any. See? A very good setting to turn on. I would say turn item component purchasing off so you don't accidentally buy something you don't want and turn auto attack off so you don't like attack when you're in bushes and stuff. Now we're going to cover how to double bind your space bar. So, you go to your hot keys. You go to camera control. Center camera on champion space bar. And save. Exit game. And close the client. Find your League of Legends file in file explorer. Uh you'll have input and persistent settings. titre: MILIO SEASON 16 GUIDE: TUTORIAL on how to level up as a SUPPORT player in LEAGUE OF LEGENDS | Tip...
transcription: On est parti sur un guide milio pour pouvoir progresser et rinder comme un fou. On est en train de regarder le deuxième milieu world afin de progresser et de vous donner tous les astuces qu'il effectue pour pouvoir progresser. Mais pour débuter, on va commencer à voir les spells. Le passif de milio va te permettre d'enchanter la personne que tu touches avec un de tes sorts avec ton Q Z E R parce que oui, ça marche avec le Q et tu vas voir ce que c'est. Ça va permettre de gagner un pourcentage de dégât bonus. Ça scale en fonction de la partie mais ça entre entre 8 et 10 % donc ulti cartus bam + 10 % si un sort sur lui le rêve en plus ça va rajouter une petite brûlure. Tonku ça va être un sort un skill shot en ligne droite qui va toucher la première cible qui va la bump et un plus afficher une zone de dégâts qui va mettre des dégâts. titre: Camera Control - League of Legends Beginner Basics - LOL Class
transcription: [Music] wow [Music] okay all right lawlers today's class is going to be all about the basics for camera control this directly impacts how you play the game in terms of the vision that you have around your character and around the map so off the top we're just going to cover off some of the hot keys and the abilities that you have to control your camera and then we'll talk a little bit more about how you actually want to go about using camera control in your game play to become a better player so off the top at the bottom right of your screen you have a toggle option here which is lock camera or unlock camera so if i click to lock this camera that's when the line is through it as i move my character around my field of view in the game is gonna stay basically fixed around my character with me always at the center of it okay if i toggle that off and unlock it that field the view remains fixed unless i move it my character movement within that field does not affect my view it only changes if i take my cursor and bump it into the sides of the map up down left right then it moves that field of vision okay the other way you can move that field of vision is by left clicking on the minimap that takes you to the different lanes okay so on top of this camera unlock lock toggle that you have your disposal and that left clicking of your mouse to move to different areas the minimap you also have some f keys that you have at your disposal too so by default i believe for most of you unless you've already adjusted your settings these f keys are tied to the enemy or sorry the ally champions on your team so for example right now if i press f2 on my keyboard that is going to quickly bring my field of vision to the bot lane tail if i press f3 it's going to quickly bring my field of vision to top lane with lux if i press f1 that's always tied to me so it brings it back to myself here in the mid lane okay you can also press space bar to bring the field of view back to your character at all times okay so space key brings it back space key brings it back okay if i wanted to again quickly see how my border top laner doing i can press the f key okay i see my kale's getting destroyed by garen and i press f3 i see my lux is doing an okay job against that vladimir all right so that is really the meat and potatoes all the basics you need to understand about camera control now we're going to get into the good stuff and really talk about why it's important to play a certain way with that camera control okay as we eat a bunch of autos from this press the attack uh ash midlane okay so off the top i'm just gonna say you should all be playing with unlocked camera if you're not playing with unlocked camera already you need to build this into your game play vision is information and information is everything in league of legends alright i know that all of you want to think that your micro play is godly and that's what's going to give you the ability to always win games it's really a lot more macro than that in league of legends and vision is one of the building blocks and fundamentals of being able to consistently play well okay so you can see right now as i play this lane i'm unlocked camera and i always have myself at the bottom left of the screen at the ash at the bottom or at the top right okay this will obviously mirror backwards if i was playing the top side of the map and the ash was playing bot side okay now the reason it's important to have unlocked camera and keep the camera set up like this so that i have myself at the bottom left and more vision on the top right where the ash is is because as i'm laning in the early game it's really important that i know what's going on with these minion waves and what's going on with the ash and if there's anybody coming from top side river to gank me or bot side river to gank me so you can see now as i'm auto attacking this wave i have the camera positioned in such a way where really i have good vision of the top side jungle and the bot side jungle or sorry excuse me the rivers so i know if anyone's coming okay now if i want to back for example here again another reason why unlock cameras better again i'm keeping both of us in the corners of the screen as i come back to my turret now and press my back button i can bump that camera up and see what's going on with the ash i can check the minion waves i could see if there's a jungler that's going to come help or clean that wave or not right so really really important to play unlock camera in lane for those reasons now the other important aspect of the unlocked camera that you need to understand in the early game is it gives you the ability to not waste vision on what i'll call a safe space so if the camera's locked i titre: The COMPLETE SMOLDER STARTER GUIDE! - League of Legends
transcription: smolder is without a doubt one of the best Champions we'll ever see for climbing solo que we expect that no matter how many Nerfs he may receive that he will always be an extremely strong Champion especially in the lower ELO brackets in this guide you will find everything you need to get started ranging from his play style a quick ability breakdown combos tips and tricks how to fight builds and much more also we're excited to announce we've done a massive update adding all brand new courses for season 14 on our website skillcap tocom and if that's not enough we upload 10 new Smurf commentaries every week where a challenger teaches you how to play every champion in the exact rank you're stuck in the best part you can try all this out completely risk-free if you don't rank up while actively using skill cap you get your money back no questions asked so what are you waiting for click the link in the description below to get the rank you've always wanted in season 14 so what type of champion is smolder well as an ADC he plays similarly to Ezreal where you're more of a Caster rather than a pure Auto attacker the nice thing is that unlike Ezreal you don't need Korean Challenger mechanics to pull him off as smolder is extremely easy for how effective he is at carrying games now the reason why we mentioned that smoulder is likely to be a Powerhouse in low ELO is because of his infinite scaling similar to Champions like vagar or aelan Soul scaling Champions are without a doubt some of the best in lower brackets and smolder is looking to be an absurd late game carrying typically these scaling Champions aren't for everyone if you think that you'll be sitting in Lane AFK trying to farm Stacks don't worry that's not the case at all you are more than capable of fighting early on sure you won't be Draven levels of strong but you're not going to AFK every Lane you can definitely take chances and look for good fights not only that but the stacking process can actually be quite enjoyable you will be deleting waves quickly while powering up in a very satisfying way so it won't feel like a chore as it does on other stacking Champions that being said stacking efficiently every game is without a doubt the most important skill to mastering smolder so towards the end of the guide we'll give you some quick and easy tips to make stacking a breeze every game for now though let's break down his abilities smolder has a stacking passive based on how many stacks he has his three basic abilities will simply deal more damage not only only that but his queue will evolve based on his Stacks but we'll get to that the way you build this up is exactly the same as the way vagar does it if you hit an enemy Champion with your abilities you will earn one stack but your primary way of farming should be by Las hitting minions with your Q why we mentioned that this won't be as annoying as on other Champions is that your Q can generate more Stacks by killing multiple targets with it and that brings us to our q and its Evolutions to start the game it is a really basic ability it's point and click damage at 25 Stacks it will evolve to deal Splash damage around the target this will speed up your stacking significantly at 125 Stacks you get a pretty big spike your Q will now send some extra Fireballs behind your target to deal roughly the same damage then at 225 Stacks your Q's final evolution occurs now hitting a target with it will apply a Max HP burn that deals decent damage not only that but if your target drops below this threshold they instantly die it's a mini Elder Dragon and it's very very strong this is an extremely powerful Spike so you want to get to this point quickly moving on to your W this spell is much simpler it's just a rolling Fireball it moves fast at first but then it slows down it also does a bit of extra Splash damage around any targeted hits so hitting people that are clumped up will deal very nice damage your e is a very strong repositioning ability you get a burst of movement speed and you're also able to fly over terrain while doing so you will rain fire down on the lowest Health target for his ultimate smolder will Roar calling his mom and puts an indicator on the ground showing the direction then she comes down in a straight line to deal damage enemies in the center will take extra damage and be slowed his mom will also heal smolder a bit if he's standing in the area it's fair to say this is a pretty high value spell with a ton of damage and utility as you can probably tell his kit is fairly simple so smolder doesn't have any complicated combos to learn playing him well is more about understanding how to properly use your abilities but let's cover the couple of simple combos he does have first you can input buffer both your q and W to make them come out a bit smoother so Q flash is a thing as well as W flash unfortunately you locked into roaring for your RS animation so you can't flash to high the direction like you can on some other Champions we figured we' mention this so you don't waste your flashes trying to be fancy the only ability that you really need to think about using is your e for starters the movement speed you get is based on your current speed this means that you ideally want to cast your e while you're sped up by something else this can be ghost or maybe the buff from an enchanter support like Lulu's w nami's e and so on the second big thing about it is that you can't do anything else while you're flying if you do you'll drop down so a very important thing you need to keep in mind is to press your q and W before you fly into the air take a look at this smolder duel for example the enemy one only uses his W before flying our smolder on the other hand uses both of his spells while in the air Q comes back off coold down giving us two rotations of our Q compared to the enemy smolder who only used one all right with his super basic combos out of the way let's talk about some gameplay tips to get the most out of his kit smolder has two problems you're going to have to learn to deal with one is his very short range to cast Q or Auto attack which are your primary damage spells you have to be really close to your target we shouldn't have to tell you why being this close to an enemy is usually not good for you on top of that our his weird Auto lockouts on a lot of other adcs a general gameplay pattern is to weave spells between Auto attacks so for example on civer you would Auto QE Auto very smoothly smolder on the other hand can't Auto smoothly after casting his Q or W take a look if you cast Q notice how long it takes for his Auto to go off after same thing with this W it just takes forever for smolder to Auto after casting however you can move immediately after these spells so what you'd ideally do is auto Q kite then Auto W kite or something along those lines basically if you play a lot of other adcs you'll want to break the habit of Auto attacking after casting a spell through ART testing we found that smolder is far less Auto Reliant than most other adcs combined with your short range these Auto lockouts do not give you a lot of time to sit there Auto attacking targets your gameplay pattern is going to be about casting spells and immediately kiting to reposition a lot of the time this doesn't mean you won't ever Auto attack of course Autos are a big part of any adc's damage but you won't be able to weave in as many as other ADC Champions as you can see though this isn't that much of a problem because you already deal so much damage now let's talk about some ways to deal with your short range having the range advantage in League can be pretty oppressive you get to dictate when fights happen poke someone from far away and so on so as smolder you may struggle unless you incorporate these tactics remember that at 25 stacks of your passive your Q will begin dealing Splash damage you achieve this Spike very quickly into the laning phase and will be one of your primary ways of trading so ordinarily your Q's range is fairly mediocre but take a look at its max range when you factor in the splash damage it becomes a very respectable ability when you play around the Splash and allows you to control a lot of space in the lane by utilizing minion waves you need to really play around this you want to Stack your Passive by spamming Q on the wave while also using that Splash damage to threaten your opponents smolder is quite good at spam shoving waves and applying pressure this way so make sure you play around the splash as much as possible at 125 Stacks your range in harass potential becomes even better and follows the same guidelines always try to keep a minion between you and your opponent to maximize your range your R is also a big tool you want to play around the problem is that although it's an extremely strong spell it can be quite easy to miss so before casting it make make sure that your opponents are out of movement abilities in Cc or are stuck next to a wall or something where they can't avoid the damage you can also look to cast it from fog of War although you might still alert them through a roar if they can't see the initial Direction on the ground it'll be much harder to know where it's coming from or where you're aiming it the nice thing about smolder is that these principles continue to apply throughout the game smolder is honestly a pretty easy Champion to play as long as you follow these fundamental concepts in fights hiding your ults direction is a great way to catch an enemy team off guard you also want to continue to take fights around minion waves or jungle camps players will definitely underestimate what you can do around waves as the game goes on take this fed Talon for example look at how smolder fishes for Q's again and again through the wave so that he can poke Talon but never be in range to be attacked eventually he deals enough damage to where he can fly in and burn him down with one final Q which brings us to one of our most important tips about smolder the draft phase we have really emphasized playing around minion wave so far and for good reason like we said smolder Auto and Q range are so small that you will either be out ranged or engaged on if you try to fight normally what this means is that you'd ideally want to avoid drafting smolder into purely ranged comps if the enemy team has four to five ranged Champions you will definitely struggle to play the game smolder will be at his best when there are two to three melee Champions on the enemy team that you can bounce qes off of to hit the enemy back line as you can see in this example as Diana chases smolder can threaten arri with his Q's spread damage you make it extremely difficult for range Champions to follow up on their melee front liners by constantly barraging them with q's and W so just keep this in mind as one of the main factors for when you will succeed or have a hard game as smolder if you're versus multiple melee Champions you will have much more opportunities and angles to bounce qes off of to spread fire everywhere this won't mean that you'll be worthless into comps with range but it will give you less opportunities to deal damage and you'll have to play around your cooldowns a bit smarter as for your build you'll likely be building very similarly to ezal as an ADC Caster you'll do this Rune page and for your items go Essence Reaver cool down boots into navori there's also the option of going the Trinity force and Mir Mana route from our testing the crit variation felt a bit better to play but that might change with item balancing one thing we want to mention is that smolder seems very similar to adcs like kgma Vain and Varys adcs with percentage based damage can typically build one to two damage items and then go into tank your options rather than continuing to build damage so if you see an enemy team composition that is countered by armor or magic resist then you can very easily adapt your build to go for much tankier options with good passive stack farming your base damage is more than good enough and you should feel very comfortable giving up some damage for survivability and that brings us to our final section where we'll give you some tips for stacking your passive an extremely important concept to keep in mind on smolder is that getting ahead will usually mean you stay ahead the more passive Stacks you have early the more Q evolutions and damage you get this lets you play more aggressively Farm more waves look for more trades and so on basically if you do well early you'll be able to keep that momentum easily and keep stacking super fast and snowball out of control on the flip side if you struggle early on and don't get many passive Stacks then you'll slowly fall behind you can't Farm as many waves take as many fights Etc and as a result you will stack your passive super slowly and not be nearly as effective as you should be what this means is that super small optimizations can add up a lot since getting to a q Evolution even just one minute faster might be the difference between a one or lost game first things first we recommend you run biscuits in your Rune page while you will eventually fix your Mana issues you won't be able to spam spells early on having biscuits to replenish your Mana when you run out will usually let you spam your queue the entire laning phase without much worry having a solid start to your passive stacking is much more easily achieved with biscuits the second thing to keep in mind is that after 25 Stacks you can obviously start farming multiple minions per with the splash damage smolder will be building a Sheen item however the sheen only amplifies the main target hit not the splash damage what this means is that when you're preparing minions to last hit you always want to prioritize casting Q on the highest health minion in the clump so for example here smolder is preparing this group of four minions the melee is the one with the highest health left so he uses sheen on it while the Lesser Splash damage is enough to get the rest next if you're one stack away from evolving your que that cast will go off as if you'd already evolved it so if you're at 24 Stacks you should try try to prepare the wave as if you've already got the splash damage this can get you a couple of extra Stacks at your Q's Evolution break points finally if you want to use your W to trade try to throw it through the Caster creeps this basically prepares them instantly to be farmed by your que and is a nice way to trade and farm at the same time and remember if you truly want to improve and rank up fast head on over to skill cap.com we just finished a massive update adding all brand new courses for season 14 we even upload 10 new Smurf commentaries each week where a challenger teaches you how to play every champion in the exact rank you're stuck in and remember you can try all this out risk-free if you don't rank up while actively using skill cap you get your money back no questions asked so what are you waiting for click the link in the description below to get the rank you've always wanted this season all right that should be absolutely everything you need to get started playing smolder immediately good luck and thanks for [Music] watching

titre: 3 Minute Nidalee Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Nitali jungle? Well, she has amazing burst damage. Her mobility is incredible. She can easily solo carry games and she has a very high skill ceiling. However, she doesn't bring any lockdown at all. And she is damage reliant struggling from behind. Niti has a few passives. Firstly, she starts with her ultimate. Secondly, while in a brush, she is ghosted and gains bonus movement speed, which is massively increased when moving towards an enemy champion. And this bonus lasts for 2 seconds after leaving the brush. Her third passive is when she lands a human form Q or W, she's granted the same movement speed buffs as though she were in a brush. And in addition, the first use of her cat form Q and W against that target is enhanced. Nidi's human form Q is a straight line skill shot which deals damage to the first enemy hit. And this deals massively increased damage based on the distance traveled. In cat form, her Q is an auto attack reset which enhances her next attack to deal bonus damage, gain bonus range, and deals even more damage based on the target's missing health. In addition, if the target's marked by her passive, the damage is increased by 30%. Her human form W places down a trap on the floor, which goes invisible after 1 and 1/2 seconds. Enemies that step on this take damage over time and are marked. Her cat form W leaps in a direction, dealing damage to enemies when she lands. And if the targets marked, she can leap even further. And if she gets a takedown in cat form, the cool down of this ability is reduced. Nidi's human E can be used on herself or an ally, which heals them a small amount and grants them a large amount of bonus attack speed. In cat form, it deals damage in a cone skill shot to all enemies hit. Niti's ultimate switches her between human form and cat form. In cat form, she is a mele champion and in human form, she is a range champion. And when she marks an enemy with a Q or W, the cooldown of this ability is reset. For combos, starting cat form and use W into R and Q. Follow up with W and E before changing back into cat form and going in with W, E, and Q before attacking away. And if you land a spear, use E on yourself, then change into cat form and go in with W into E and Q before attacking away. For matchups, Nidley can struggle into pure jewelist, so you're always happy to fight her. However, she's great into champions she can easily invade and assassinate. For runes, take this. Dark harvest is great burst damage. For build order, start red monster and pot into a lichbane and tier 2 boots. After this, a shadow flame and zonas are great pickups before finishing a build with any of these as needed. For skill order, start Q, then E, then W before maxing Q, then E, then W, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and smite. Starting the game, you almost always want to do a full clear. Nidi's early ganks are strong, so you can clear one side and go for a gank. But honestly, her clear is so fast, you may as well do the full clear. From here, you are a very gank heavy jungler, and when looking for ganks, always consider what CC your team brings, what escapes the enemy has, and where is the lane push to. Landing your human form, Q or W is incredibly important to get that long dash and easy kills. Enter in the Midame, you're all about picks and objectives. Set out your traps and land spears to mark enemies before jumping in and exploding them and converting into those objectives. Entering late game team fight, place the backline mage waiting for your chance to jump in. Use E on yourself or a carry and look to land your trap or your spears. You essentially want to keep dealing damage from the backline until you can mark one of their carries. And once Mark, jump in and assassinate
