titre: 3 Minute Darius Guide - A Guide for League of Legends
transcription: Why play Darius top? Well, he is a massive lane bully. He is a resetting hyper carry in a team fight. He's great in a 1v2 situation and he just brings amazing AoE damage. However, he is a mobile relying on ghost and flash and he can get kited by range champions. Darius's first passive is each of his attacks and abilities apply a stack of bleed onto the enemy. The bleed lasts for 5 seconds and can be stacked up to five times. Darius's second passive is when he fully stacks his bleed five times on an enemy or kills someone with his ultimate, he becomes empowered. While empowered, he gains an insane amount of attack damage and instantly applies five bleed stacks to anyone he attacks. Darius's Q, after a short delay, deals damage in a circle around him. Enemies hit by the outer edge are dealt more damage and applied a stack of bleed. Where enemies hit by the inner part take less damage and don't get a stack of his passive. Darius also heals for each champion or large monster hit. Darius's W is an auto attack reset, which empowers his next attack to have bonus range, deal bonus damage, and slow the target. If this kills the target, half the cool down is refunded and the mana cost. Darius's E has a passive where he gains a large amount of percentage armor pen. The active is a cone skill shot which pulls enemies towards him, making them airborne and slowed. Darius's ultimate is a point-and-click ability which causes him to leap towards that target and deal true damage. This deals 20% additional damage for each bleed stack on the target. If this kills the target, Darius fears nearby minions and monsters and also we can recast the ability within the next 20 seconds. And at level 16, this has no cool down if it kills an enemy. For combos, start with an auto attack into W and then your Q. Follow up with an attack into E before attacking into your alt. And if you start with E, auto attack into W. Follow up with Q into two attacks into your alt. For matchups, Darius is great at meanly top laners who he can just walk up to and bully. However, he can struggle against top laners who can kite him and space him outside of his range. For runes, take this. Conqueror has amazing synergy with his passive. For build order, start shield and pot into tier 2 boots and a stridebreaker. After this, a Sterax and Dead Man's are great pickups before finishing a build with any of these as needed. For skill order, start W, then Q, then E before maxing Q, then E, then W, taking alt whenever you can. For summoner spells, take flash and ghost. Start in the game, you want to bully early and bully hard. With your passive, you simply deal more damage than most other champions at this point in the game. And if you can simply walk up to your opponent and attack W them, they'll be slowed as you can keep stacking. And if you do get to five stacks, you have so much attack damage, you can just kill them. At level two, your Q gives you even more kill threat. And at level three, being able to pull an enemy back in that doesn't want to be in a fight is just a death sentence. Remember, if you have to start the fight with E, go for it. But if there's any way you can avoid it, pulling someone back in when they don't want to fight you just gives you easy kills. And of course, once you get level six, fully stacking your passive on someone is very easy kills. Entering the mid game, take your tower as soon as possible. From here, you're solid as a split pusher or grouping up for fights. So, push out the side lane for pressure and then either keep pushing or group up with your team. Entering late game team fights, you want to play a frontto back play style. Attack anything in front of you with a massive focus on stacking five bleed stacks on one target to trigger your passive. Once fully stacked, you're looking to put more bleed passives on as many champions as possible. And then as soon as they're low enough, just start dunking. And that's how to play Darius Top. If this video helped you, leave a like and comment below who you want to see

titre: How To Fix League of Legends Stuck On Tutorial Patching Screen (2025)
transcription: So, if you're playing League of Legends here or at least you're trying to learn League of Legends and you're stuck on the tutorial page here or stuck in the tutorial patching screen, well, there are a few things that you may want to know here and how League of Legends actually work and why you're you may be encountering this problem. Now, first things first, we need to understand that League of Legends uh by default or by nature here uses and downloads a lot of data. Now, usually League of Legends like uh is usually around like 15 if not saying like 30 to 60 gigabytes. So, that's the amount of space that you need just to play League of Legends. Now, usually if that uh if you haven't downloaded the full uh package here or if you haven't fully uh downloaded League of Legends, well, it will first start patching itself. Now, in some cases, you may have the option to exit this page. Now, sometimes start now here would be grayed out. In some cases, if a like for example a tutorial here is available, you should see the start now button. Now, in most cases here, if you don't want to do the tutorial and just skip over into the actual act uh the action itself, well, you can just click on the X button. But in some cases, that would also be disabled as well. And also in some cases, the play button would also be disabled. So, how do you fix that? Well, that usually happens because there were some new updates applied on or inside the game. So, honestly, I've frequently experienced this with League of Legends, which is kind of weird, but the way I fix this is you just need to fully close Riot and as well as League of Legends. So, how do you do that? So, clicking the X button at the top right here is not enough in some cases. So, what you need to do is you want to go and press the following. It's going to be control shift escape on your keyboard or you can go to the search bar here at the very bottom and just type in task manager. Now once you are in task manager you want to go and just scroll down over here look for anything League of Legends related. Now once you do like pro see processes for League of Legends what you need to do is you want to go ahead and just right click on them. So yeah as you can see League of Legends and there's a lot of them. So you want to go and right click on the ones that has the highest memory here. Right click on it. Click on end task. Just wait for a while here. Now you want to get go ahead and just head over to task manager again. And next is you want to go and just look for anything related to Riot. So usually it's going to be at the very top here. So Riot client, right click on it. Click on the end task. Now just to make sure that the process is or League of Legends is fully closed down. Go to the bottom right here. You should see the show hidden icons section. And if you do see Riot Games or Riot Client is still in here, just right click on it, click on exit to fully exit it uh from or to fully close it from your PC. Now, once you have this fully closed, you can go and just uh close this window, open up Riot Client again, and run or boot up League of Legends again and see if that actually helps. Now, if that doesn't help, you may want to restart your PC or your laptop and see if that solves the issue. But that's about it. So hopefully this video was able to help you like and subscribe but to our channel.

titre: COSPLAY TUTORIAL: Spirit Blossom Ahri's Nine Tails from League of Legends
transcription: Salut les gars, Tiffany ici avec un tutoriel de cosplay sur la façon dont j'ai fabriqué mes queues Spirit Blossom Ahri. Et voici une image de référence d'Ahri dans le jeu de League of Legends. Et pour commencer, nous allons avoir besoin d’un plan. Et devine quoi? J'en ai un pour toi. Donc, si vous souhaitez utiliser mon plan de queue pour confectionner votre costume Ahri, vous pouvez le trouver sur mon site Web, TiffanyGordonCosplay.com ou simplement consulter le lien ci-dessous. Une fois que vous avez obtenu votre plan, imprimez-le, collez-le ensemble et découpez-le pour obtenir la forme de la queue. Et une fois que le plan est entièrement découpé, je l'ai ensuite appliqué sur un tissu en fausse fourrure blanche, j'ai obtenu le tissu de Joann, puis j'ai découpé la forme de la queue 9 fois, puis je l'ai retourné et j'ai fait la même chose du côté opposé. Donc au total, vous devriez avoir 18 pièces de queue. Cependant, vous aurez probablement besoin d'environ trois mètres de tissu pour ce faire. Ensuite, j'ai attrapé un morceau de chaque côté de la queue et je l'ai épinglé à l'envers, laissant l'extrémité de la queue ouverte. Je l'ai ensuite cousu ensemble à l'aide de ma machine à coudre et d'un seul point, encore une fois, en laissant l'extrémité de la queue ouverte et j'ai répété le même processus pour les neuf queues. Passons maintenant à la partie amusante, retourner chacune des queues à l’envers. Yay! Cela a en fait pris beaucoup de temps, je vous recommande donc de regarder un film tout en faisant cela. Lorsque vous retournez les queues à l’envers, vous aurez une couture très visible. Et pour vous débarrasser de la couture, vous devrez utiliser une sorte de brosse à perruque et tirer simplement la couture de haut en bas dans toutes les directions. Ainsi, toute la fourrure sortira entre vos coutures et se mélangera uniformément. Maintenant, ajoutons de la couleur aux queues. Pour ce faire, vous aurez besoin d’eau très chaude. J'ai donc fini par utiliser ma chaudière à eau chaude, en remplissant mon seau environ au tiers, puis j'ai ajouté un peu de savon à vaisselle. Et pour notre première couleur que nous avons essayé de créer était la couleur bleu pierre précieuse, qui nécessitait deux tasses et demie de bleu saphir et deux cuillères à soupe de colorant synthétique graphite RIT. Je dis tenté parce que j'ai essayé de faire deux couleurs avec la queue, en faisant une plus foncée en bas et une plus claire en haut et la couleur la plus foncée, qui était celle-ci. Je n'ai pas laissé le tissu suffisamment longtemps pour que la couleur ne soit vraiment pas visible. Je recommanderais de laisser votre tissu dans une couleur plus foncée pendant au moins une heure, plutôt que les 20 minutes que j'ai faites pour chaque queue. Quoi qu'il en soit, j'ai fini par mettre la partie inférieure de ma queue dans la teinture, en la trempant de haut en bas, de cette façon, elle avait une sorte de bord mélangé, donc elle ne ressemblait pas à une strie complète sur le bas. Et pour la deuxième et principale couleur, cela a fonctionné à merveille. J'ai fait le même processus avec de l'eau bouillante, juste du savon à vaisselle. Et pour cette couleur de teinture synthétique RIT que nous avons utilisée, c'était la couleur sarcelle tropicale. Et lorsque le minuteur a été terminé sur le premier bain de teinture, j'ai ensuite retiré la partie inférieure de la teinture du tissu, puis je l'ai plongé et immergé dans notre nouvelle couleur de bain sarcelle tropicale pendant 20 à 30 minutes. Encore une fois, je recommande de faire cela plus longtemps pour obtenir une couleur plus vibrante. Une fois que vous avez terminé le deuxième bain de teinture, rincez toute la teinture à l'eau froide jusqu'à ce que vous ne voyiez plus aucune couleur provenant de l'eau, puis répétez le processus pour les neuf queues et mettez chacune d'elles. un à l’extérieur pour sécher toute la nuit. Une fois que tous les morceaux de queue étaient secs, je suis ensuite revenu avec des peintures à l'aérographe, en faisant un mélange d'une couleur noire, bleue et turquoise de la peinture à l'aérographe du testeur sur les parties inférieures de la queue. Dans ce résultat, j'étais beaucoup plus heureux. Et vous pouvez voir ici sur cette image l’avant et l’après. L' étape suivante consiste à démêler toute votre fourrure sur la queue, et vous pouvez simplement utiliser une brosse à perruque pour ce faire. Vous voulez peigner tous les cheveux dans une direction allant de la base de la queue à la pointe, de cette façon, ils semblent un peu plus naturels, comme la queue d'une véritable créature. Et pour leur donner un peu plus de volume aux queues. J'ai fini par les bourrer de fibre de polyester. Je recommande de ne farcir qu'un tout petit peu chaque queue. Faites attention à ne pas trop les surcharger, car tout cela finira par ajouter du poids à l'ensemble des queues. Pour les structures osseuses de chacune des queues. J'ai utilisé des tuyaux en CPVC, que j'ai coupés à la longueur de la queue à l'aide d'une scie, puis j'ai mis le tuyau sur une de mes queues, en marquant de quel côté j'allais le plier. Ces marques vous serviront de lignes directrices lorsque vous reviendrez avec un pistolet thermique, afin que vous sachiez où appliquer la chaleur et où plier le tuyau. Et puis à l’aide d’un pistolet thermique et de gants résistants à la chaleur, j’ai ensuite appliqué la chaleur à l’endroit où je l’ai marqué sur les tuyaux en CPVC. Et puis, lorsque le tuyau est prêt à être plié, il deviendra un peu plus argileux et deviendra probablement un peu brunâtre. Et puis, vous savez, vous pouvez le plier puis le maintenir avec les deux mains en place jusqu'à ce qu'il refroidisse pour durcir dans la nouvelle forme souhaitée. Et voici à quoi cela ressemblait. Après tout, neuf des structures osseuses étaient pliées. Et pour fixer les tuyaux CPCV au harnais que nous allons réaliser, j'ai fini par utiliser un coude à 90 degrés. C'est ce dans quoi votre tuyau va s'insérer, ainsi que la plus petite partie va s'insérer dans votre harnais. Pour coller ces pièces ensemble, vous devrez rendre rugueux les bords du tuyau en CPVC. De cette façon, votre époxy aura quelque chose à quoi s'accrocher, puis mélangerez votre époxy en deux parties et votre colle en place, en lui faisant épouser la même courbure avec la direction de chaque queue de la même manière. Pour la conception du harnais, j'ai fait la partie inférieure en forme de fer à cheval avec neuf trous pour que chacune de nos structures de coccyx puisse s'insérer à l'intérieur, et pour la partie supérieure, j'ai fait une forme en forme de sangle qui va passer en dessous. le corset du Spirit Blossom Ahri, et il a deux trous de chaque côté, pour que je puisse faire passer les sangles. J'ai fini par le modéliser en 3D dans un programme de CAO appelé Rhino six et je l'ai imprimé en filament ABS. Je noterai que vous n'êtes pas obligé d'imprimer le harnais en 3D. Cependant, vous aurez besoin de quelque chose qui sera extrêmement solide en raison du poids des neuf queues. Je recommanderais donc d'utiliser du plexiglas, du bois ou quelque chose qui peut être vraiment solide et robuste. Lorsque la pièce a été imprimée, j'ai ensuite vérifié les trous de chacun de mes raccords et j'ai décidé que je voulais plier un peu plus les tuyaux en PVC, pour avoir des angles un peu différents. J'ai donc répété la méthode de chauffage au pistolet thermique sur les canalisations pour fixer les canalisations au harnais. Je l'ai alourdi avec des sacs de 2 livres, puis j'ai appliqué une très grande quantité d'époxy à l'intérieur de chacun des trous et autour de chacun des angles de 90 degrés sur les tuyaux, en me déplaçant lentement, en m'assurant que chacun est étalé. uniformément. Et voici à quoi cela ressemble une fois toutes les pièces collées. Pour les sangles, j'ai fini par me procurer des sangles en nylon ainsi que deux boucles, et ces boucles spécifiques ne s'ajustent que d'un côté, j'ai donc dû coudre une moitié de la boucle sur la sangle. Ensuite, j'ai inséré mes sangles là où j'avais les trous dans le harnais, puis j'ai lentement glissé chacune des queues sur chacun des tuyaux. Et c’est ainsi que j’ai réalisé ma configuration de queue Ahri. Merci beaucoup les gars d'avoir regardé ce tutoriel de cosplay sur la façon dont j'ai créé mes queues Spirit Blossom Ahri et j'espère que vous l'avez apprécié et assurez-vous d'appuyer sur le bouton J'aime. Appuyez sur le bouton d'abonnement à la chaîne et je vous verrai pour notre prochain tutoriel cosplay. Beaucoup d'amour les gars !

titre: GUIDE YUUMI SAISON 16 : TUTO comment MONTER SUPPORT sur LEAGUE OF LEGENDS | Tips & Combos, Runes
transcription: On est parti sur un guide Yumi. On est là pour t'expliquer comment tout exterminer avec Yumi grâce à une Yumi qui est actuellement bah l'une des meilleures Yumi d'Europe. Tout d'abord, on va commencer avec les spell. La partie qui c'est qu'elle peut se définir un meilleur ami. Ce meilleur ami va être défini en fonction bah sur la personne qu'elle est le plus accrochée et qu'elle va lesquel va toucher le plus de sorts. Donc deuxième passif, ça va être quand elle va infligé des dégâts puis va mount ou être mount sur son meilleur ami. Cette personne va recevoir un h. Le kumi, ça va être un sort à tête chercheuse. C'est-à-dire qu'il va suivre ta souris. Tu vas pouvoir faire des petites bouques, un peu des slalom comme frère un carte et tu peux toucher l'adversaire grâce à ça en contournant. Ça va slow l'adversaire, ça va lui faire des dégâts. Si tu n'es pas mon sortra en ligne droite et si tu as touché l'adversaire et que tu es sur ton meilleur ami, ton meilleur ami gagnera des effets on hit bonus. Ton Z, c'est un sort que tu auras directement dès le début de la partie. Tu pourras avoir deux sorts au niveau 1 et c'est le fait de t'attacher sur quelqu'un. Ça va te permettre d'avoir un bonus de h et de shield et aussi d'apporter de la régen de vie on hit à ton allié comme peut faire le col l'abatteur. Lei ça va un peu fonctionner comme le shield de karma. Tu vas pouvoir te shield toi si tu es un mount ou l'allié s'il est moun et en plus tu vas apporter un bonus de vitesse d'attaque. Ce qu'il faut savoir c'est que ça va aussi rendre de la mana en fonction de la mana manquante de ton allié. Et ton R ça va être plusieurs salves comme plein de petits R. de Sona, mais ils ne vont pas stun, ils vont seulement ralentir et ça va régénérer énormément la personne sur laquelle tu es lié et beaucoup plus sur ton best friend. Et voilà pour les sorti. Maintenant, à propos du gameplay sur la phase de lane, tu vas commencer comme ça. Et pour continuer ta game, ça va être très simple. [Musique]

titre: How to Start League of Legends in 30 Minutes? Complete Beginner's Guide
transcription: Salut YouTube, c'est NKP. Je suis passé de Silver à Master en seulement un an et demi. C'est pour ça que je vous fais cette vidéo sur un guide débutant pour les personnes qui sont pas encore niveau 30 ou les personnes qui sont enfer en bronze, en silver et en gold. Vous allez voir les gens sont paqués. Galio retourne caler mon R. Elle l'a pas elle l'a fait stone. Elle a le R, elle a le R. Elle a le R. Magnifique a encore flashé sur les cinq. Voilà regardez Wade, on chope cinq mecs charmés. On cale le Q le E. Elle vient de route quatre mecs, elle vient de les tuer et c'est tout ce que tu dois chercher avec ses raffines pour solo carryer des games. C'est pour ça que là en 3 4 contre 5, ils sont arrivés à solo caril fight parce qu'en fait c'est un champion qui a la capacité de mettre un ulti Leona sur toute la team adverse et d'avoir un impact de taré. Voilà en tout cas c'est tout pour le guide de Séraphine. C'est la grande difficulté c'est de jouer la ligne phase et après c'est juste arriver à trouver le potentiel en fight en jouant correctement ses combos et ce build peut vraiment peut-être t'aider. Donc c'est rare que je vous parle de build mais en vrai c'est la première fois que je vois quelqu'un faire ça et j'aime bien le concept parce que ça complète énormément le pic. Donc voilà, en tout cas rappel, on est en live tous les jours de 9h à 15h. N'hésitez pas à nous rejoindre. Cit sur ce ciao ciao. Le passif de Séraphine consue au fait que tous les trois sorts, on un indicateur visuel sur elle, elle va pouvoir dupliquer le prochain sort qu' qu'elle utilise de base, son, son Z ou son E. Et aussi à chaque fois qu'elle utilise un sort ou que des alliés utilisent des sorts autour d'elle, elle va gagner de plus en plus de dégâts sur sa prochaine autoattaque ainsi qu'une porté améliorée. Le queue de Séraphine, ça va être un sort de zone. En fait, c'est très similaire au que de Soraka niveau animation, niveau utilisation. Tu vas tirer le sort, ça va partir en détonation dans une zone et ça va faire des dégâts. Ça infligera plus de dégâts en fonction des HP manquants des joueur qu'elle va toucher. Le Z de séraphine, ça va être une immense zone de shield sur les alliés. C'est comme le Z en fait de Sona comme on a fait un guide Sona, nésitez pas à aller le voir. Sauf que la zone, elle est trois fois plus grande. Elle va donner des shields et des boost speed. Mais tu sais, je t'ai expliqué qu'avec ses rapines, son massif, ça double ses sort et c'est la particularité du champion. Si c'est raffine quand elle utilise ce sort et qu'elle est shield, elle va avoir un deuxième effet qui va permettre de healer tous les gens en AOE comme un peu une rédemption autour d'elle. Donc ce qui va se passer c'est que si tu utilises ton Z si tu as ton passif, tu te shieldas, tu tu boosteras mo speed de tout le monde et tu reshielderas tout le monde. Mais vu que tu es déjà shield et ben tu vas encore plus il au lieu d'avoir un shield, tu en auras pas deux, tu auras deux shields et un très puissant. Le séraphine, ça va être un mini skill shot en zone qui va partir en ligne droite, va traverser les unités et qui va les ralentir. Petite particularité toujours avec le passif, c'est un slow mais si la personne est déjà slow, c'est un route. Et si la personne est déjà route, c'est un stun. Donc c'est pour ça que ou si la personne est stun, c'est aussi un stun. C'est pour ça que tu vas vouloir toucher bah ton double E ou caler ton E sur quelqu'un qui prend un slow. Donc tu regardes ta draft, tu vois un Jack qui peut stun, tu peux mettre ton E. Tu vois une C, tu peux mettre ton E. Tu vois un galo qui roue, tu peux mettre ton E. Ce qui fait que ton E mono va devenir en stone si tu follow correctement. Donc la malou est x 3 x 4 en comparaison un stone. Le air de séraphine, on se rend compte que c'est un peu un copiercollé sur Sona petit à petit. C'est un ultime de zone un peu comme un air Sona avec quand même une plus grande largeur et une beaucoup plus grande profondeur. Et la particularité c'est que ça rebondit sur les champions. C'estàd que le R peut quasiment traverser toute la carte. Si ça touche des alliés ou des ennemis, ça va rebondir sur tes alliés ou sur tes ennemis. Donc si tu as un allié qui est devant toi et quelqu'un qui est encore derrière adversaire, tu peux ultime ton allié et ça va prolonger ton ultime pour toucher l'adversaire. Voilà globalement. Et c'est aussi un sort que tu peux air flasher qui est très intéressant mécaniquement pour faire des belles mécaniques.

titre: How to Move League of Legends to Another Drive (Step by Step)
transcription: in this video I'll show you how to move League of Legends to another Drive first up close this one make sure that it is turned off at a taskbar as well right click on it and hit on exit if you can't see it right here and you want to make sure that it is closed right click on a blank space right here on your taskbar and select task manager maximize this one and make sure that right games is not running right here in the background all right now once done go ahead over to your file explorer then local dis C as this is usually where riot games are installed now let's go ahead over to Riot games then click on League of Legends right click on it then hit on cut all right if you want to move it if you just want to copy just go ahead and hit on copy but as the title suggest we're going to move it so I'm going to hit on cut now once that's done go ahead over to the prefer drive for example here drive D I'm just going to right click on a blank space then hit on paste after that let's just wait for your process to finish as you can see here the process is done next step would be is to open up right client just locate right client right here in your desktop and if you can't see it just go ahead and go to your search bar and type in right client all right hit open now in here go ahead over to League of Legends click on it and as you can see it will require us to install and what we're going to do here is just basically click on install all right click on it now as you can see here there will be a popup now what we're going to do here is just basically locate already installed right here at the very bottom click on it and it will open up file explorer so what we're going to do here is just find the drive where you have transfer the game all right as you can see here I'm just going to click on League of Legends hit okay and as you can see here as fast as that you'll be able to just simply click on play and enjoy the game all right and that's the fastest way to move League of Legends to another Drive well I hope this video helps and I'll see you in the next one

titre: Every Riftbound Rule Explained! How To Play The League Of Legends TCG
transcription: Riftbound is here and I've been having an absolute blast playing it. But while the game seems super simple on the surface, the rule book is a massive 65 pages and I know you don't have time to read it, so I did it for you. In this video, I'll be breaking down the entire rule book so you'll be ready for any situation you come across. So, with that said, let's start with deck building. To play Riftbound, you need two decks, a champion legend and battlefields. Your champion legend is placed in the legend zone at the start of the game and dictates the domain identity of your deck. Basically, the colors or runes that you are allowed to play. For example, let's take a look at Jinx Loose Cannon. You can see at the top left corner of her card that she has a red and purple icon. These represent the Fury and Chaos domains, respectively. This means that your deck can only consist of cards from those domains. And notably, if a card has two domains, your deck's legend must match both of them to use that card. The other domains include calm, which is green, mind, which is blue, body, which is orange, and order, which is yellow. Once you've selected your legend, you can build your main deck. This will consist of at least 40 cards and includes your units, gears, spells, and chosen champion unit. Let's start there. Your chosen champion will be placed in the champion zone at the start of the game. They must have a tag that matches your champion legend. For example, if Jinx Loose Cannon was your legend, you can use either Jinx Demolitionist or Jinx Rebel as your chosen champion. As you can see that they have the Jinx tag, but you need to be a bit careful here. We are specifically looking for champion units for this. So, if you have Annie as your legend, you can't use Tibbers as a chosen champion despite it having the Annie tag because it is a signature unit and not a champion unit. Which leads us into our next topic, signature cards. Your deck can only have at maximum three signature cards and they must share a tag with your champion legend. So, if hypothetically Annie had Tibbers and another signature card of some kind, you would have to choose some combination of the two cards that would add up to at most three copies. Otherwise, all cards in your deck will have a cap of three each, and there's not really any other big restrictions to worry about. The other deck is the rune deck. As the name suggests, this is specifically for your runes, and they must match your domain identity, and you must have 12 of them. You also need to provide battlefields as well, but the specific number depends on the mode that you are playing. Typically, in a one versus one game, though, you will provide three. With that, you are ready to build a deck. The board is divided into several zones. The base is the main location that each player may play their units and gear to, as well as the player's runes, but you might see these allocated to their own area for clarity in some boards. However, they are technically in the base. The battlefield zone, as the name suggests, are where battlefields are placed, typically up to one per player. This area has a subzone called the faceown zone as well. This is a place where a single card can be placed face down through effects like hidden. Players can only place cards into the faceown zone if they control the associated battlefield. And if the player loses control of that battlefield, their card is removed afterwards. The last area that we need to talk about on the board is the legend zone. This is where your champion legend is placed. It isn't considered to be a location, and the legend placed there can't be removed or displaced during the game for any reason. Then we have our nonboard zones. The trash is basically your graveyard. Cards go there when they've been killed, destroyed, discarded, or after a spell has been used. Your cards can never for any reason be put into the trash of another player. For example, if they control your unit when it is destroyed, it will be returned to your trash instead of theirs. The trash can be reorganized and ordered freely. However, it is also public information, so you can't hide it from your opponents if they ask to see it during a game. Similarly, we have banishment. This is where cards go if they are banished by some card effects. Basically, a place for cards removed from the game where they are harder to recover than if they were just in the trash. This area is also public information. And speaking of information, next we have the hand. While the cards in your hand are private, the number of cards is public. And so your opponents can ask how many cards you're holding at any given time. On that note, all items on the board in the collective play area are public information, and players can ask to view or be provided with the details of any face up card in play. This isn't the Yu-Gi-Oh anime. We got to tell the other players the truth if they ask about our cards. The state of those cards is also public information. For example, if a unit is buffed or if a gear is exhausted. The champion zone is where your chosen champion goes. You can play that champion normally at any point in the game by paying its regular cost. However, once a champion leaves the zone, they typically can't be returned there. So, if you play your chosen champion and it is killed, it will go to the trash. Then we have the main and rune deck zones respectively. And that's it. So now we have our decks and we know the boards. Now let's talk about game objects. Game objects is a term that mostly refers to your cards. Firstly, each type of card will have a unique backside depending on which deck they are placed in. Main deck cards, rune deck cards, and battlefields or champion legends. However, the more important part is definitely on the front. Let's look at and break down the main deck cards. Each card in the main deck will have a cost depicted in the top left corner. However, there are two types of costs that a card might have. The first is the energy cost. This is the number that you see in the big circle. Directly below that, you will sometimes see a few rune symbols. These are power costs. And we'll talk about how to pay these and the difference between them in a little bit. The bar across the middle of the card shows us the card's name. And again, this name has a limit of three in your deck. Notably, this refers to the full name of the card as well. So, Jinx Demolitionist and Jinx Rebel are unique cards despite both being Jinx. Cards can also have categories and subcategories as well. Permanence are cards from the main deck which remain on the board after being played, like units and gear. Meanwhile, spells are a subcategory that do not remain on the board. The section below a card's name is the rules text. This can include abilities, instructions, keywords, flavor text, or be completely empty. But let's get into the nitty-gritty here. On unit cards, you will sometimes find tags which represent which champion, region, faction, or species that they belong to. For example, if we look at Dune Drake, we can see that it has the Shereimma and the dragon tag. This typically doesn't have any value in and of itself. However, some cards like Herold of the Scales will interact with or have synergy tied to a specific tag. In the top right corner of unit cards, we will see their might. Looking back at Dune Drake, and you can see that they have a might of five. When a unit is dealt damage, they are marked for that amount. And if that amount ever equals or exceeds their might, they are killed. Might is also the amount of damage that a unit will do to another unit in combat. In this sense, might is both their attack and health points all in one. Damage is removed in two specific situations. Firstly, at the end of each player's turn, and second, at the end of any given combat. Also, a unit smite can never go below zero, if that ever becomes relevant. When a unit enters the board, they will do so exhausted unless affected by accelerate or similar effects. This means they are tapped or put horizontal and effectively used up or spent for the turn. This will last until they are readied on that player's following turn or until they are readied by another effect. If a unit has an activated ability of some kind, it can be activated during that player's action phase. Though, this can't be used in response or as a reaction and has to be done in an open game state unless told otherwise. But while not every unit has an activated ability, all units have a shared inherent ability to perform what is called a standard move. Again, this can only happen in an open game state. Players can perform multiple standard moves simultaneously. However, all units moving must be going to the same destination, though their origins don't need to be the same. The cost of making a standard move is to exhaust that unit. Now, there are a few restrictions to movement, though. Firstly, units may move from their base to a battlefield or the battlefield to their base, but they can't move from one battlefield to the other unless some ability like ganking or a spell specifically allows it. On top of that, units cannot move to a battlefield that has units belonging to two other players there already. This, of course, is only relevant in game modes that have three or more players. Our next game object is gear. This can only be played in a player's base, and if it was somehow brought to a battlefield, it would be immediately recalled to the base. Like units, gear can have activated abilities, which can be used in open states. However, unlike units, gear enters readied. They are super simple cards for the most part, so luckily we don't have to spend much time on them. Following gear are spells. These can be activated in open game states, and once it is used, its effect will be triggered, and then it will be sent to your trash. spells resolve the rules text from top to bottom. And once it begins resolving, nothing else can interrupt or be resolved until all of the effects on the card have been completed. However, there are two keywords which do allow other players to chain onto spells. And we will get into chains a little bit later. The action keyword allows a spell to not only be played in open game states, but during showdowns as well. Meanwhile, the reaction keyword can also be played during all forms of closed states. We'll talk about the chains in depth later, as well as game states. However, in general, when a chain is created, the newest spell will resolve and then you will work backwards towards the oldest spell in that chain. Runes are another subcategory which are channeled rather than played. However, while they do typically stay on the field, they are not considered permanence as they do not come from the main deck. Runes are the primary resource used to pay costs in Riftbound and can produce both energy and power. By exhausting a rune, you can produce one energy, which can be used to pay for your main deck cards or activated abilities. Meanwhile, to produce power, a rune can be recycled to the bottom of the rune deck. Notably, though, they can only be used to produce power of their associated domain or generic power. Energy and power created by your runes is stored in the rune pool and will remain there until it is spent or until the current turn or draw phase ends. Our next game objects are battlefields. These each tend to have their own passive or triggered abilities which will affect the units and spells that interact with that location. For example, Sigil of the Storm will trigger when you conquer it and make you recycle a rune. Meanwhile, the arena's greatest passively gives each player a point during their first beginning phase. While battlefields are technically part of your deck, they do not get shuffled into your deck at the start of the game and only exist to be selected from during setup. These locations can't be moved or killed in most cases, and you can stack as many units that you'd like to on any one battlefield. However, there is only one spot to place a hidden card on each of them. Next, we have the cornerstone of your entire deck, your champion legend. These typically are not played throughout the game, can't leave the legend zone, can't be killed, but can be targeted by spells or game effects. These cards can have passive, triggered, or activated abilities, and each has one or more domains, though to date, each example has two domains. We also have game objects that aren't actually cards in your deck and instead are created throughout the course of the game. These are called tokens. If you create a token, you can use pre-established cards to represent them. However, if you don't have token cards, you don't need to worry as anything can be used to represent these tokens. You can use coins, dice, hell, if you can get your cat to sit still long enough, you can even use them as a token if you really want to. They are not technically considered cards and don't have costs or domains. However, they can have tags, types, mites, or any other property which will be listed on the effect that created that token. All right, we've covered all the game objects. Now, let's talk about control. This applies differently depending on the game object that we are talking about. Battlefields can either be controlled by a specific player or uncontrolled. If a player is the only person to have units on a battlefield, they control it. This control can be contested when a unit controlled by a player who doesn't control that battlefield moves onto it and will remain contested until control is established or reestablished. Each player will provide three battlefields, which will be included in their deck list, though not part of their actual deck during the game. Of these, one will be randomly selected from each player and placed into their respective battlefield zone. To start the game, the player going second will channel an extra rune during the first channel of the game. And so, they will start with three runes while the player who goes first only has two. One v one matches are basically the same, however, you are playing a best of three rather than just one. You'll play the first game, the winner of which will get one point, and then move on to the second. However, when choosing which battlefields to use this time, you will not be able to use the battlefield that was selected in the first round. So, each player will randomly choose from the two that they have remaining. If things go to round three, the final battlefield each player provided will be used. Otherwise, all the same rules. Our next format is called skirmish, and this is a three-player free-for-all. Again, each player provides three battlefields, and one will be randomly selected from each pile. This mode slightly differs in how the first round is played though as the first player does not draw this time on their first turn. Meanwhile, whoever is going third gets the extra rune, and the second player essentially plays normally, drawing a card on their first turn and only channeling two on the first turn. War is the four player free-for-all format. This one adds yet another difference. While each player still provides a randomly chosen battlefield, the player who goes first will remove their battlefield from the game. So, despite having four players, we will only be using three battlefields. Other than that, the first player still doesn't draw, and the last player gets extra starting runes. The second and third players play as normal. And our final format is Magma Chamber, a 2v2 game mode. This mode is played to 11 points, and just like war, we will be using three battlefields selected in the same way. We will also be using the same first round mechanics as war as well. No draw for the first player and an extra rune for the last player. Where things differ a little bit is turn order as the turn player will alternate between teams. Team A, team B, team A, team B, and then looping. But this mode adds a few unique rules as well. Players can cast spells during their teammates's turn. You will only score points during your own control battlefield during the beginning step, not your partners. On top of that, control is not shared. You can't hide cards at battlefields controlled by your teammates, nor can you typically move your teammates's units. However, if a card targets a friendly unit, you can target your teammates cards. Notably, your hand is still private. You can openly say whatever you want, but you can't actually show your teammate your hand. Lastly, the final point has a bit of a change as well. If you are getting your final point by conquering, you must score each battlefield in the same turn with the exception of battlefields occupied by your teammate at the turn's beginning phase. Now that we know the different ways that you can play the game, let's talk about how not to play the game with conceding. Players can give up at any time and when they do so, they are removed from the game. If there is only one player remaining, that player wins. If not, the player who conceds removes themselves from any showdowns, banishes all of their permanence and runes from the game, and on top of that, their battlefield is also removed from the game. If that battlefield was being used, replace it with a token with no abilities. However, any hidden cards at that location being removed do not get transferred to the token. After that's all done, proceed with the game as usual. Okay. From here, we're going to talk about layers. Layers are the system in which game effects will alter the traits, intrinsic abilities, or properties of a game object. Basically, anytime multiple effects are applying to one object, layers will give you clarity on how they will work. In general, there are three layers: trait altering effects, ability altering effects, and arithmetic. Trait altering effects deal with granting, removing, or replacing inherent traits of a game object. These include things like name, super type, type, tags, controller, cost, and domain. Assignment of might is also dealt with in this layer. This specifically refers to effects that say a unit's might becomes X for the turn rather than saying things like a unit gains one might. Another trait altering effect is one game object becoming a copy of another, in which case all traits and rules text is replaced or added to the original object as specified by the effect being used. Trait altering effects can be noted by phrases like becomes, gives, is, or are. For example, an ability saying something like other friendly units are yortals. The next layer is ability altering effects. This layer deals with granting, removing, and replacing the abilities or rules text of game objects. These include keywords, passive abilities, appending or removing rules text, or duplicating rules text from one game object to another. These effects can be identified with things like becomes, gives, loses, have, has, is, or are in their text. For example, other friendly units have ganking. And our final layer is arithmetic, which deals with increasing or decreasing numerical values of the traits on game objects. Things like might, energy costs, or power cost. This one is pretty straightforward. If more than one effect applies to the same object in the same layer, then both will apply, but the order will be determined by dependency. A dependency is created in the following circumstances. Applying one of the effects alters the existence of another. applying one of the effects alters the number of objects that the other can influence or if applying one of the effects alters the outcome when applying the other. If multiple effects apply to the same layer but no dependency is made, then they are simply applied in the order of being played. Oldest first, newest last. And lastly, let's talk about keywords and anything else you might need to know before playing. I'll put these in alphabetical order so that it will be easy to find whatever you're looking for. Keywords are abilities that appear on cards as shorthand for specific effects or abilities, which are great for established players, but can be overwhelming for new ones. You can see keywords on cards as having colored highlights behind them. Accelerate is a unit ability which states that when you play that unit, you can pay one energy and one power as an additional cost to ready them when they enter. This cannot be paid after the unit has already been placed on the board and can only be used when paying the card's cost to play it. Action is a permissive keyword which can be found on a variety of cards. Action allows the cards to be played or activated during showdowns, even if it isn't the controlling player's turn. However, cards with the ability are not restricted to showdowns only and can be played at any time those cards or abilities could normally be used as well. Assault is a passive keyword for units that will always be followed by a numerical value. If you don't see a number, the assault value is one. While that unit is attacking, it will gain might equal to the assault number. For example, assault 3 gives three might. If a unit with assault is granted another instance of assault, the values are added together. Death Nell is a triggered ability on permanence that activates when the permanent dies. It will be written as death nail dash and then the relevant effect to be triggered when that unit is killed. Each death nail on a card will trigger separately if they have multiple and the controller of that card will choose the order in which they are used. Deflect is a passive ability formatted the same way that assault is being followed by a numerical value. Deflect says that spells and abilities an opponent controls that target a unit with deflect must pay an amount of power equal to the deflect value as an additional cost. So if you target a card with deflect 2, you will need to pay two power. Now notably the power required to pay this extra cost is not any specific domain and any power can be used to fulfill it. Similar to assault, if a unit with deflect is granted more deflect, the totals are just added together. Ganking is a passive ability on a unit which allows them to move from one battlefield to another. This ability is an added permission to the standard move ability that all units have. Hidden is something we covered a bit earlier, but I'll repeat it here. This is a prerequisite keyword to hide a card, which can be seen on some spells, units, and gear. The hidden keyword says that rather than pay for the normal cost of a card, you can pay one power to play that card face down at a battlefield that you control that doesn't already have a card hidden there. Starting on the next player's turn, that hidden card gains a reaction ability, allowing you to play it while ignoring its base cost, so long as the timing and targets are legal. Notably, a hidden card can still be played for its normal cost at any normal timing that it could be used at with no further restrictions on targeting. Legion is a conditional keyword which becomes active if you have played another main deck card earlier in that turn. Reaction, like action, is a permissive keyword which allows you to play a card or ability during closed states on any player's turn. Shield is a static ability on units that is written with a numerical value after it in a similar way to assault and deflect. And additional sources of shield will add to this value in the same way as those keywords. This activates when the unit is a defender and increases their might by the shield value. Tank is a passive ability on units that makes it so that that unit must be assigned lethal damage before another unit that player controls can be targeted for combat damage. If multiple tanks are present, the opposing player can assign damage to either of them freely, but non-tank units still can't be targeted for damage. Vision is a triggered ability on permanence that says, "When you play this, look at the top card of your main deck and then you can recycle it if you'd like to. If multiple cards with vision are all triggered at once, the instances of vision will be applied separately and do not stack into one action. This means if you choose to not recycle the first card on the first instance of vision, you will effectively continue to look at the same card in the future iterations unless you change your mind and do decide to recycle it. But that is the entirety of the core rule book summarized and explained. With this in hand, you should be pretty much able to understand every part of the game and you are officially ready to play. This took quite a long time to go through everything, so if you enjoyed, remember to leave a like and subscribe. it would be very much appreciated.

titre: FIX League of Legends Stuck On Tutorial Patching Screen (2026)
transcription: Hello everyone. Today we will solve the problem where League of Legends gets stuck on the tutorial patching screen for a long time. Before we begin, it is important to understand that seeing the game stuck on a patching screen does not always mean there is an error. It might just be taking longer because the full game of League of Legends can take up to 16 to 30 GB of space. This means it is downloading a very large file and the time it takes depends on your internet speed. To check if it is really stuck or just slow, make sure your internet connection is working fine. To help speed it up, you can try stabilizing your internet using a few commands in Windows. Open the Windows search bar and type cmd. Right click on command prompt and select run as administrator. When the command prompt window opens, type the following commands one by one and press enter after each command. These commands will clear your DNS cache, reset your IP settings, and fix any network socket errors. After you run them, restart your computer, and open League of Legends again. Now, give it some time to download the rest of the files. As long as you can see data being downloaded in the launcher, it means it's working. Once the download finishes, the button you see will change, and you will see a button labeled start now. That means your game is ready to play. Thanks for watching.

titre: How to Make League of Legends Cinematics | Two Minute Tips
transcription: [Music] in two minutes i'm going to demonstrate how you can direct and record your own league of legends cinematics to begin you'll need to download league director this is the tool that will allow you to access all the things you need to record professional cinematics you can find it linked in the description below next run the program the first thing you'll need to do is make sure that the path is pointing to the correct location of your league of legends files if so you'll now need to go to the cfg file it is pointing to so we can enable replay api find it right click it select open with notepad under general type enable replay api equals one exactly as shown here now to create a cinematic you need to choose a replay from your or someone else's match history download it and open it up once the game begins you should notice that league director has now opened some new windows with a variety of options to choose from before you do anything there are a couple of steps we need to take to make things easier for you first change the camera option from the bottom left to fps camera you should now have the ability to pan the camera by holding the left mouse button and to move the camera using the numpad keys 8 4 5 and 6. if you do not have a numpad follow the guide linked in the description to rebind the keys next set graphics quality to very high otherwise some features with leak director will not work finally go to key bindings and set keyframe position keyframe rotation and keyframe field of view to the same hotkey turn off all visibility options above short environment so that everything looks clean choose your first camera angle and then hit the button you assigned to the three camera options previously then play until you find your next point pause and set up your camera again after that hit that camera hotkey button again and then tick apply sequence press play sequence and watch your cinematic in action if you're happy with the result go back to league director to the recording window make sure all your settings match the ones on screen and choose an output directory that you would like your video to be saved to press record sequence and wait for the recording to complete just like that you should now have a custom made league cinematic [Music] did you find this useful be sure to experiment with league director and all of its settings in order to get full use out of the software in the meantime be sure to like and share the video and subscribe to the channel you can follow the british esports association on all of our social channels using the links in the description below thank you for watching and enjoy the rest your day

titre: How Riot Let League of Legends Become A Toxic Mess
transcription: League of Legends is insanely toxic. Teamwork, anonymity, and 40-minute matches breed frustration, and Riot knows this. They've worked on punishing usage of slurs, added mute buttons, hid the names of teammates in champion select, and even axed the bait ping to curb toxicity. But while chat flames get silenced, a darker problem fers. Gameplay sabotage. Soft ining. Giving up on games or deliberate throws are very commonly seen in games. These silent trolls ruin matches without typing a word and go unpunished. Riot system punishes words fiercely but lets toxic actions slide. Today we will explore how Riot fought to control toxicity and why it's so hard to punish toxic gameplay which has made League of Legends become a toxic mess. Over the years, Riot has built a comprehensive toolkit aimed at silencing verbal abuse, focused on measures that only tackle chatbased toxicity. In the early years of the game, their work to battle toxicity revolved around a player-based system called the Tribunal that existed from 2011 until 2014. The Tribunal was a communitydriven disciplinary system introduced by Riot Games to address toxic behavior in League of Legends. It allowed players to review reported cases of misconduct. Reviewers were presented with chat logs, game statistics, and report details to determine whether to punish or pardon the accused player. Punishable offenses include hate speech, intentional feeding, and offensive summoner names based on the majority verdict. Punishments ranged from warnings to permanent bans with more severe cases reviewed manually by Riot's player support team. Despite its innovative approach, the tribunal was disabled in early 2014. The system was slow and inefficient in nature and it had issues with accuracy, particularly due to natural human biases. Since 2014, Riot has transitioned to automated systems capable of issuing penalties within minutes with more constant enforcement of punishments. Riot has introduced many tools to combat toxicity. Most commonly used is the forward/mute or forward/ full mute command, which allows players to mute individuals or everyone in the game, teammates and opponents. chat and pings to totally block out any communication in an instant. A newer tool is the /deafen command that prevents you from chatting and will also stop you from seeing text from other players, but also notifies other players on your team that you are deafened. These options are very controversial since players sometimes go full mute or deafen at the beginning upon making a mistake or to restrict themselves from flaming and in essence block out their whole team. This means they cannot listen to pings to communicate, cannot make calls or coordinate plays, and it kind of defeats the whole purpose of the game, which revolves around five players working together to win. Riot also introduced champion select anonymity in ranked solo duo, replacing summoner names with neutral camp aliases, Gromp, Raptor, Scuttle Crab, and so on. This was implemented with the idea that players cannot search the names of the players on sites like OP.gg GG and see their win rate, if they're on a losing streak, if they're autofilled, or if they're just first timing a champion. In essence, it eliminated the option to prejudge players based on past performances. Another tool is Riot's instant feedback system, which scans chat logs and hands out rapid penalties up to 14day bans for hate speech, slurs, and other severe violations, often issuing chat or ranked restrictions within minutes of a match. This swift automated enforcement has driven a noticeable decline in visible slurs and abusive language. But it applies only to typed offenses, leaving disruptive in-game actions unressed. The honor system was a smart design by Riot to reward positive behavior by granting loot, hexte chests, even passes, and the season's victorious skin depending on if you have a certain level of honor. And if you get chat restricted once, it knocks you down several tiers, locking you out of loot or other free perks. But it's not been rare to see a player who's been soft ining, not trying, and ruining games still be at honor 5. While this solution is effective to an extent, this approach does nothing to deter players who silently troll, and they can still reach and maintain high honor levels as long as they never type an offensive word. In addition to punitive measures, Riot has made symbolic adjustments intended to curb misuse of in-game tools. For example, the bait ping was removed entirely after players weaponized it to mock and antagonize teammates. If someone misplayed, players would question mark ping them. But with the new bait ping, which looked like a hook, it was used to bully people for their mistake, signaling them to hang themselves. Riot's arsenal of chat focused tools. Full muting commands, ping restrictions, rapid chat bans for slurs, honaggated rewards, and even champion select anonymity has succeeded in silencing visible flame. But it's left players trapped in a silent battlefield where the true threat goes unnoticed. Teammates who quietly sabotage matches through their actions rather than their words. With the easy option to mute or disable all communication, verbal abuse has been driven underground. Yet muted trolls can still grieve objectives, feed kills, play isolated, and stall games for 30 to 40 minutes, all without so much as a single offending message. Riot's anti-toxicity measures have created a quiet chat window, but failed to curb the most destructive form of toxicity. Silent gameplay sabotage that no mute button can stop. This silent sabotage often takes the form of soft ining, subtle, deliberate misplays that fly under the radar. A player might accidentally dive under enemy towers, wonder the map without joining fights, ignore crucial objectives as if they didn't matter, and refuse to help teammates altogether. On the surface, it looks like a bad match. But when it happens again and again every three games, or even worse, 10, 20 games in a row, it reveals a pattern of willful grief. Detecting soft ining is a nightmare for automated systems built to flag hateful language or blatant AFK behavior. How do you tell if a zero and 15 Yasawa was just having the worst game of his life or intentionally feeding to ruin his team's climb? How can you prove that the jungler got frustrated early and decided to grief your lane, smite your cannon and steal your waves? Riot's instant feedback system and reporting tools aren't equipped for this gray area. So, most soft ining players sail through unpunished, continuing to spread frustration with every silent misstep. You could not mute this kind of behavior. You could not play around it. And if frustration you write to them in the chat, there's a bigger chance of you getting punished for typing instead of them for ining. I'm addicted. In reality, most would rather endure a toxic flamer who they can just mute than be stuck with a silent sabotur for 40 minutes. But why are these issues so hard to combat? And what makes League of Legends not only frustrating to play sometimes, but insanely toxic as well? League of Legends very design and community culture lay the groundwork for toxicity by demanding long team reliant commitments. Average matches stretch over 35 minutes where you're tied with four other individuals and everyone depends on each other, which means a single disruptive player can ruin an entire game and spark toxicity. Losing such a substantial time investment to a teammate who intentionally ruins games can be mentally draining, plunging players into an emotional state known to every gamer as tilt. Players tilt when a 50/50 play goes south or when their teammate does not do what you expect them or need them to do. So, expectedly, the fact that you have to play and coordinate with four other strangers, all with unique play styles, ideas, and short calls can make games feel disjointed and frustrating. And while this mechanic of coordination between five random players is where a lot of the appeal for the game comes from, it is also a place where most of the frustration from the game emerges. This frustration sometimes translates into what is known as surrender mentality. This mentality in essence is when games go south early. Players give up and want to surrender so they don't get stuck into unwininnable games. While surrender can be a useful escape for players stuck in hopeless situations. Its low threshold and ease of use have fostered a toxic mindset. Instead of rallying for a comeback, many prefer to cut their losses in which they even flame the player who would like to try and win and not give up. and the players who wish to fight on feel pressured to give up when faced with even minor setbacks. Riot has worked hard to combat this kind of behavior. Within their more recent updates, they said that they will introduce an improved detection algorithm which will scan for disruptive gameplay patterns such as repeated Norton 10 scores, consistent AFK split pushing, and suspicious objective neglect and automatically flag accounts for review. Second, Riot introduced transparency notifications, letting players know when and who of the ones they reported has received a penalty. Despite these ideas, many players question whether these tools can reliably distinguish genuine bad luck from intentional trolling. Can an automated system avoid banning someone who truly had a rough day? But that is truly a problem that cannot be really detected to 100% accuracy by any system as there isn't and there cannot be clear benchmarks for intent. The risk is still there that the system may either overpunish honest mistakes or underpunish calculated sabotage. But it's a step in the right direction as these changes not only promote bigger transparency, it also informs the League community that Riot is aware and is also working to combat the silent sabotages. League of Legends has long been plagued by toxicity. Whether it's the players who flame in chat or the silent saboturs, the game has struggled throughout the years with this issue. While Riot Games has made strides in curbing overt verbal abuse, the more insidious issue of gameplay sabotage remains inadequately addressed. To restore faith and ensure a fair gaming environment, Riot must take decisive steps. It has to establish clear criteria and define and publicly share the standards used to identify and penalize gameplay sabotage. They may have to implement hardware IP bans for repeat offenders and not ban just the account but the player altogether. While the idea of you getting notified which player was banned from your report is great, Riot can take things further by providing some kind of transparency reports, monthly or quarterly reports detailing the number of actions taken against gameplay offenses, which will maybe show the players that their reports and their voices matter. While it's unrealistic to expect Riot to eliminate all instances of trolling, soft ining, and other forms of sabotage, proactive measures can significantly mitigate their impact. With transparent detection methods, stricter repeat offender penalties, and regular public reports, Riot can begin to restore trust and improve the league experience. These steps will not only deter disruptive behavior, but also reassure players that their concerns are being addressed, contributing to a more enjoyable experience for the entire community. League's toxicity isn't just about words. It's about 40 minutes of your life being wasted by a troll Riot refuses to ban. Players often wonder, if they can detect a slur in seconds, why can't they detect 10 games of running it down mid? And it's a valid question. And until they fix this, League will remain a toxic mess and players will keep quitting.

titre: COSPLAY TUTORIAL: Spirit Blossom Ahri's Nine Tails from League of Legends
transcription: Salut les gars, Tiffany ici avec un tutoriel de cosplay sur la façon dont j'ai fabriqué mes queues Spirit Blossom Ahri. Et voici une image de référence d'Ahri dans le jeu de League of Legends. Et pour commencer, nous allons avoir besoin d’un plan. Et devine quoi? J'en ai un pour toi. Donc, si vous souhaitez utiliser mon plan de queue pour confectionner votre costume Ahri, vous pouvez le trouver sur mon site Web, TiffanyGordonCosplay.com ou simplement consulter le lien ci-dessous. Une fois que vous avez obtenu votre plan, imprimez-le, collez-le ensemble et découpez-le pour obtenir la forme de la queue. Et une fois que le plan est entièrement découpé, je l'ai ensuite appliqué sur un tissu en fausse fourrure blanche, j'ai obtenu le tissu de Joann, puis j'ai découpé la forme de la queue 9 fois, puis je l'ai retourné et j'ai fait la même chose du côté opposé. Donc au total, vous devriez avoir 18 pièces de queue. Cependant, vous aurez probablement besoin d'environ trois mètres de tissu pour ce faire. Ensuite, j'ai attrapé un morceau de chaque côté de la queue et je l'ai épinglé à l'envers, laissant l'extrémité de la queue ouverte. Je l'ai ensuite cousu ensemble à l'aide de ma machine à coudre et d'un seul point, encore une fois, en laissant l'extrémité de la queue ouverte et j'ai répété le même processus pour les neuf queues. Passons maintenant à la partie amusante, retourner chacune des queues à l’envers. Yay! Cela a en fait pris beaucoup de temps, je vous recommande donc de regarder un film tout en faisant cela. Lorsque vous retournez les queues à l’envers, vous aurez une couture très visible. Et pour vous débarrasser de la couture, vous devrez utiliser une sorte de brosse à perruque et tirer simplement la couture de haut en bas dans toutes les directions. Ainsi, toute la fourrure sortira entre vos coutures et se mélangera uniformément. Maintenant, ajoutons de la couleur aux queues. Pour ce faire, vous aurez besoin d’eau très chaude. J'ai donc fini par utiliser ma chaudière à eau chaude, en remplissant mon seau environ au tiers, puis j'ai ajouté un peu de savon à vaisselle. Et pour notre première couleur que nous avons essayé de créer était la couleur bleu pierre précieuse, qui nécessitait deux tasses et demie de bleu saphir et deux cuillères à soupe de colorant synthétique graphite RIT. Je dis tenté parce que j'ai essayé de faire deux couleurs avec la queue, en faisant une plus foncée en bas et une plus claire en haut et la couleur la plus foncée, qui était celle-ci. Je n'ai pas laissé le tissu suffisamment longtemps pour que la couleur ne soit vraiment pas visible. Je recommanderais de laisser votre tissu dans une couleur plus foncée pendant au moins une heure, plutôt que les 20 minutes que j'ai faites pour chaque queue. Quoi qu'il en soit, j'ai fini par mettre la partie inférieure de ma queue dans la teinture, en la trempant de haut en bas, de cette façon, elle avait une sorte de bord mélangé, donc elle ne ressemblait pas à une strie complète sur le bas. Et pour la deuxième et principale couleur, cela a fonctionné à merveille. J'ai fait le même processus avec de l'eau bouillante, juste du savon à vaisselle. Et pour cette couleur de teinture synthétique RIT que nous avons utilisée, c'était la couleur sarcelle tropicale. Et lorsque le minuteur a été terminé sur le premier bain de teinture, j'ai ensuite retiré la partie inférieure de la teinture du tissu, puis je l'ai plongé et immergé dans notre nouvelle couleur de bain sarcelle tropicale pendant 20 à 30 minutes. Encore une fois, je recommande de faire cela plus longtemps pour obtenir une couleur plus vibrante. Une fois que vous avez terminé le deuxième bain de teinture, rincez toute la teinture à l'eau froide jusqu'à ce que vous ne voyiez plus aucune couleur provenant de l'eau, puis répétez le processus pour les neuf queues et mettez chacune d'elles. un à l’extérieur pour sécher toute la nuit. Une fois que tous les morceaux de queue étaient secs, je suis ensuite revenu avec des peintures à l'aérographe, en faisant un mélange d'une couleur noire, bleue et turquoise de la peinture à l'aérographe du testeur sur les parties inférieures de la queue. Dans ce résultat, j'étais beaucoup plus heureux. Et vous pouvez voir ici sur cette image l’avant et l’après. L' étape suivante consiste à démêler toute votre fourrure sur la queue, et vous pouvez simplement utiliser une brosse à perruque pour ce faire. Vous voulez peigner tous les cheveux dans une direction allant de la base de la queue à la pointe, de cette façon, ils semblent un peu plus naturels, comme la queue d'une véritable créature. Et pour leur donner un peu plus de volume aux queues. J'ai fini par les bourrer de fibre de polyester. Je recommande de ne farcir qu'un tout petit peu chaque queue. Faites attention à ne pas trop les surcharger, car tout cela finira par ajouter du poids à l'ensemble des queues. Pour les structures osseuses de chacune des queues. J'ai utilisé des tuyaux en CPVC, que j'ai coupés à la longueur de la queue à l'aide d'une scie, puis j'ai mis le tuyau sur une de mes queues, en marquant de quel côté j'allais le plier. Ces marques vous serviront de lignes directrices lorsque vous reviendrez avec un pistolet thermique, afin que vous sachiez où appliquer la chaleur et où plier le tuyau. Et puis à l’aide d’un pistolet thermique et de gants résistants à la chaleur, j’ai ensuite appliqué la chaleur à l’endroit où je l’ai marqué sur les tuyaux en CPVC. Et puis, lorsque le tuyau est prêt à être plié, il deviendra un peu plus argileux et deviendra probablement un peu brunâtre. Et puis, vous savez, vous pouvez le plier puis le maintenir avec les deux mains en place jusqu'à ce qu'il refroidisse pour durcir dans la nouvelle forme souhaitée. Et voici à quoi cela ressemblait. Après tout, neuf des structures osseuses étaient pliées. Et pour fixer les tuyaux CPCV au harnais que nous allons réaliser, j'ai fini par utiliser un coude à 90 degrés. C'est ce dans quoi votre tuyau va s'insérer, ainsi que la plus petite partie va s'insérer dans votre harnais. Pour coller ces pièces ensemble, vous devrez rendre rugueux les bords du tuyau en CPVC. De cette façon, votre époxy aura quelque chose à quoi s'accrocher, puis mélangerez votre époxy en deux parties et votre colle en place, en lui faisant épouser la même courbure avec la direction de chaque queue de la même manière. Pour la conception du harnais, j'ai fait la partie inférieure en forme de fer à cheval avec neuf trous pour que chacune de nos structures de coccyx puisse s'insérer à l'intérieur, et pour la partie supérieure, j'ai fait une forme en forme de sangle qui va passer en dessous. le corset du Spirit Blossom Ahri, et il a deux trous de chaque côté, pour que je puisse faire passer les sangles. J'ai fini par le modéliser en 3D dans un programme de CAO appelé Rhino six et je l'ai imprimé en filament ABS. Je noterai que vous n'êtes pas obligé d'imprimer le harnais en 3D. Cependant, vous aurez besoin de quelque chose qui sera extrêmement solide en raison du poids des neuf queues. Je recommanderais donc d'utiliser du plexiglas, du bois ou quelque chose qui peut être vraiment solide et robuste. Lorsque la pièce a été imprimée, j'ai ensuite vérifié les trous de chacun de mes raccords et j'ai décidé que je voulais plier un peu plus les tuyaux en PVC, pour avoir des angles un peu différents. J'ai donc répété la méthode de chauffage au pistolet thermique sur les canalisations pour fixer les canalisations au harnais. Je l'ai alourdi avec des sacs de 2 livres, puis j'ai appliqué une très grande quantité d'époxy à l'intérieur de chacun des trous et autour de chacun des angles de 90 degrés sur les tuyaux, en me déplaçant lentement, en m'assurant que chacun est étalé. uniformément. Et voici à quoi cela ressemble une fois toutes les pièces collées. Pour les sangles, j'ai fini par me procurer des sangles en nylon ainsi que deux boucles, et ces boucles spécifiques ne s'ajustent que d'un côté, j'ai donc dû coudre une moitié de la boucle sur la sangle. Ensuite, j'ai inséré mes sangles là où j'avais les trous dans le harnais, puis j'ai lentement glissé chacune des queues sur chacun des tuyaux. Et c’est ainsi que j’ai réalisé ma configuration de queue Ahri. Merci beaucoup les gars d'avoir regardé ce tutoriel de cosplay sur la façon dont j'ai créé mes queues Spirit Blossom Ahri et j'espère que vous l'avez apprécié et assurez-vous d'appuyer sur le bouton J'aime. Appuyez sur le bouton d'abonnement à la chaîne et je vous verrai pour notre prochain tutoriel cosplay. Beaucoup d'amour les gars !

titre: Can You Play League of Legends on Linux in 2025? (Full Guide + Tips)
transcription: So, can you actually play League of Legends on Linux in 2025? Well, the short answer is no. Rank Games introduced their antiche system, Vanguard, into League, and this completely changed the game for Linux users. See, Vanguard works at the kernel level, meaning it has deep system control over your PC. Because of that, it's just not compatible with Linux environments. Back in the day, before patch 14.4, for tons of Linux players could still enjoy League using tools like Wine, Proton, or Lutris since those compatibility layers allowed Windows only games to run smoothly on Linux. But after Vanguard's integration, those methods stopped working entirely. Now, if you try to launch the game, it usually crashes immediately or fails during startup because Vanguard detects it's not running on a supported system. That means there are no longer any reliable workarounds, no hidden tricks, and no scripts that can bypass the checks. For the Linux community, the reality is clear. If you want to keep playing League of Legends, you'll need to either dual boot with Windows or run a separate Windows machine because Vanguard simply won't allow the game to operate under Linux. The antiche design is intentional. It's there to block anything that could interfere at a low level, and Linux just doesn't pass that requirement. So, unfortunately, the days of playing League directly on Linux are over. Thanks for watching and if this breakdown helped clear things up, don't forget to drop a like, subscribe, and I'll see you in the next

titre: BECOME A JUNGLE SMURF IN LEAGUE OF LEGENDS! SEASON 16! YOU'LL NEVER BE LOW ELO AGAIN

titre: How To Play League of Legends on Mac!
transcription: What up y'all? Going to be showing you how to play League of Legends on your Mac today. This is going to work for Apple Silicon Maxs, meaning anything with the M series chip in it. It will also work for Intel Macs. However, League of Legends says they are switching from OpenGL to Metal over the next couple of updates. I know some Intel Macs can run Metal. However, I'm not an expert on the Intel Mac, so I would just Google that real quick to make sure your Mac will be able to run League of Legends in the future. Also, here's the minimum specs that they recommend. You can pause that if you want to look at it. And with that being said, let's get into it. So, it's fairly simple. No fancy workound or extra steps you really got to do. Just go to Google. Normally, I would leave a link, but I guess League of Legends is region specific. So go to Google, type in League of Legends Mac download. Now mine's going to come up with North America. You can find whichever one you need anyways. So all you got to do is click that, click download Mac installer and allow. So then we can go to our downloads folder and doubleclick. Hit open. Now you can change where you want it to install at by hitting advanced options. I would just leave it in your applications though and then you can hit install. It will disappear, but after a minute or two, the Riot Games client will pop up. It will be updating as you can see in the bottom right. If you don't have an account, you can create an account or sign in with your username and password right here. Once you've logged in and this is finished downloading, you can hit play. It'll verify League of Legends. That will exit and League should pop up in a second. There it is. Just give it a minute to load up. And here was the notice I was talking about. graphics update. Sometime in the next few patches, we will move Mac OS users from OpenGL graphics technology to Metal Graphics. This will roll out gradually with the ability to revert back to OpenGL for a period of time until OpenGL is removed. If you need to switch from OpenGL to Metal or Meta to OpenGL, open client settings by clicking the settings wheel in the upper right hand corner. Select game and then graphics technology you can select. So, hit okay. Let's do that real quick. in the upper right hand corner, settings, go down to game, and it's on automatic. Going to put it on metal, and done. And once you've done that, you're pretty much good to go. The game probably still going to be downloading. You can see in the upper leftand corner what percentage you're at and how much longer it's going to take. For me, it's going to be another 12 minutes. But once you've done that, you can just hit play and enjoy the game. And that's about it. See you. [Music]
